text: While there is a ready [[Coterie]] enemy at Congress Chamber, increase the cost of each asset played by each investigator at Congress Chamber by 2.

name: Coterie Library
text: [action]: Reduce the cost of the next asset you play this round by 2. You may exhaust a [[Conspirator]] asset to treat this ability as a [fast] ability instead of an [action] ability for this activation.

name: Coterie Library
text: Forced - After you fail a skill test while investigating Coterie Library: The nearest [[Coterie]] enemy with an attached key shifts each of their attached keys.

name: The Key Reliquary
text: [reaction] After you successfully investigate The Key Reliquary: Instead of discovering clues, choose an unempowered key attached to an investigator at your location. Place a resource (from the token pool) on that key, as an empowerment; that key is empowered (if it is shifted, its controller may spend the empowerment instead of flipping it to its [[Unstable]] side).

name: The Key Reliquary
text: The Key Reliquary gets +2 shroud for each [[Unstable]] key in play.

name: The Knotted Tower
text: [reaction] After a location is placed adjacent to The Knotted Tower: Move to that location (from The Knotted Tower) .

name: Gravity-Defying Climb
text: Forced - When a concealed mini-card around Gravity-Defying Climb is exposed: If it is... - ...the next in the sequence listed on The Towering Vertex, it remains in play and exposed, and you may expose another concealed mini-card. - ...any other mini-card, flip it and each other concealed mini-card facedown (without shuffling them) .

name: The Towering Vertex
text: Forced - When a concealed mini-card around The Towering Vertex is exposed: If it is... - ...the Mimetic Nemesis's mini-card, deal the Mimetic Nemesis 3 damage, then flip all concealed mini-cards facedown and shuffle them facedown. - ...a decoy, it remains in play and exposed.

name: Mimetic Nemesis
text: Massive. Cannot make attacks of opportunity. Mimetic Nemesis gets +3 [per_investigator] health. Forced - When Mimetic Nemesis would take 1 or more damage from an attack or player card: Choose and reveal a concealed mini-card. If it is a decoy, cancel all damage just dealt (the decoy remains in play and faceup). Otherwise, the damage is dealt as normal, then flip all revealed mini-cards facedown and shuffle them.

name: Coterie Agent (A)
text: Concealed 2. Forced - After Coterie Agent enters the shadows: Place 1 doom on it. [reaction] When Coterie Agent is exposed: Discard it.

name: Coterie Agent (B)
text: Concealed 2. Forced - After Coterie Agent enters the shadows: Place 1 doom on it. [reaction] When Coterie Agent is exposed: Discard it.

name: Coterie Agent (C)
text: Concealed 2. Forced - After Coterie Agent enters the shadows: Place 1 doom on it. [reaction] When Coterie Agent is exposed: Discard it.

name: Conspiracy in Red
text: Revelation - Place 1 doom on an enemy in the shadows. If there are no enemies in the shadows, search the encounter deck and discard pile for an enemy with the concealed keyword and draw it. Shuffle the encounter deck.

name: Pinch in Reality
text: Peril. Revelation - You must either (choose one): Spend 1 clue or choose and discard half of the non-weakness cards in your hand (rounded up).

name: Heavy Rain
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (3). For each point you fail by, you must either take 1 horror, or place 1 of your clues on your location.

name: Coterie Envoy
text: Aloof. Patrol (nearest location with a concealed mini-card and no Coterie Envoy) While Coterie Envoy is ready, concealed mini-cards at its location cannot be exposed via player card effects. [reaction] After you defeat Coterie Envoy: Look at the revealed side of any concealed mini-card in play.

name: In Plain Sight
text: Revelation - Attach to an enemy in the shadows without a copy of In Plain Sight attached. If you cannot, In Plain Sight gains surge. Forced - After you expose attached enemy: That enemy attacks you and gets +2 fight until the end of the round. Discard In Plain Sight.

name: Knives in the Dark
text: Revelation - Put Knives in the Dark into play in your threat area if there is no copy of it in your threat area. Otherwise, this card gains surge. Forced - After you expose a decoy: Take 2 damage. (Limit once per round.) Forced - At the end of your turn: Test [agility] (3). If you succeed, discard Knives in the Dark.

name: Undercover
text: Revelation - If there are no enemies in the shadows, Undercover gains surge. Otherwise, test [intellect] or [agility] (4). If you fail, place a decoy at the location with the most concealed mini-cards, flip it facedown, and shuffle each concealed mini-card at that location.

name: Seeing Shadows
text: Revelation - Put Seeing Shadows into play in your threat area. Forced - After you fail a skill test while at a location with a concealed mini-card: Take 1 horror. [action][action]: Discard Seeing Shadows.

name: Figures in the Dark
text: Peril. Revelation - If there are no enemies in the shadows, Figures in the Dark gains surge. Otherwise, you must decide (choose one): - Each enemy in the shadows attacks you. - Each investigator loses an action.

name: Coterie Enforcer (A)
text: Concealed 1. Forced - After you expose an enemy in the shadows not named Coterie Enforcer, if Coterie Enforcer is in the shadows: It attacks you.

name: Coterie Enforcer (B)
text: Concealed 1. Forced - After you expose an enemy in the shadows not named Coterie Enforcer, if Coterie Enforcer is in the shadows: It attacks you.

name: Coterie Assassin (A)
text: Concealed 1. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if Coterie Assassin is in the shadows: It attacks each investigator at each location with 1 or more concealed mini-cards.

name: Coterie Assassin (B)
text: Concealed 1. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if Coterie Assassin is in the shadows: It attacks each investigator at each location with 1 or more concealed mini-cards.

name: Swift Retreat
text: Revelation - If there are no [[Coterie]] enemies in play, Swift Retreat gains surge. Otherwise, find the nearest [[Coterie]] enemy and test [agility] (3). If you fail, that enemy attacks you. If you fail and that enemy is not in the shadows, resolve its concealed keyword (if able) .

name: Bound in Red
text: Revelation - If there are no [[Coterie]] enemies in play, Bound in Red gains surge. Otherwise, attach it to the [[Coterie]] enemy with the highest printed health. Attached enemy gets +1 fight, +1 evade, +1 damage, and +1 horror. [action]: Flip a [[Stable]] key you control to its [[Unstable]] side. Then, discard Bound in Red. An investigator at any location may trigger this ability.

name: Key Charge
text: Surge. Revelation - Each [[Coterie]] enemy shifts each key attached to it.

name: Paracausal Entity
text: Hunter. Forced - When Paracausal Entity engages you: Look at the top card of your deck. If it is not a weakness, set it aside, out of play, as a hollow.

name: Apocalyptic Presage
text: Forced - When Apocalyptic Presage enters play: Each investigator chooses a non-weakness card they control in their hand or play area and sets it aside, out of play, as a hollow. [reaction] When Apocalyptic Presage is defeated: Instead of adding it to the victory display, discard it and choose up to 3 set-aside hollows. Add them to their owners' hands.

name: Substance Dissimulation
text: Revelation - For each set-aside hollow you own, if there are 1 or more copies of that hollow in your play area and/or hand, discard those copies and take 1 damage and 1 horror. If no cards are discarded by this effect, choose a non-weakness card you control in your hand or play area and set it aside, out of play, as a hollow.

name: Otherworldly Mimic
text: Aloof. Hunter. Forced - If Otherworldly Mimic is ready, when an investigator at its location plays, commits, or triggers an ability on a copy of a set-aside hollow: It attacks that investigator.

name: Memory Variant
text: Revelation - Put Memory Variant into play next to the agenda deck. Forced - After an event is played: Instead of discarding it, set it aside, out of play, as a hollow. [action]: Test [willpower] (4). If you succeed, discard Memory Variant. Any investigator may trigger this ability.

name: Secrets Lost
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (3). For each point you fail by, choose a non-weakness card in your hand and set it aside, out of play, as a hollow. If you succeed by 2 or more, you may take 1 horror to choose a set-aside hollow and add it to its owner's hand.

name: Paradigm Effacer
text: Aloof. Hunter. Forced - After you end your turn at Paradigm Effacer's location: Look at the top card of your deck. If it is not a weakness, set it aside, out of play, as a hollow. Then, if you own 3 or more set-aside hollows, Paradigm Effacer attacks you.

name: Matter Inversion
text: Revelation - Attach to an [[Outsider]] enemy without a copy of Matter Inversion attached. If you cannot, Matter Inversion gains surge. Attached enemy loses aloof, gains retaliate, and gets +1 fight and +1 evade. Forced - After attached enemy is successfully evaded: Discard Matter Inversion and draw 1 of your set-aside hollows.

name: Emissary from Yuggoth
text: Concealed 2. Hunter. Massive. Forced - After you discover a clue at a location with 1 or more concealed mini-cards, if Emissary from Yuggoth is in the shadows: It attacks you.

name: Tenebrous Eclipse
text: Revelation - Put Tenebrous Eclipse into play next to the agenda deck. No more than 1 concealed mini-card can be exposed by each investigator each round. Forced - At the end of the round: Discard 1 copy of Tenebrous Eclipse from play. (Max once per round.)

name: Splintered Space
text: Revelation - Attach Splintered Space to your location and test [agility] (3). If you fail, take 1 damage for each [[Hex]] treachery attached to your location. After this test ends, if there are exactly 3 copies of Splintered Space in play, discard them.

name: Beyond the Pale
text: Revelation - Attach Beyond the Pale to your location and test [willpower] (3). If you fail, take 1 horror for each [[Hex]] treachery attached to your location. After this test ends, if there are exactly 3 copies of Beyond the Pale in play, discard them.

name: Warped Reality
text: Revelation - Attach to your location. Forced - After you end your turn at attached location: Choose a non-weakness card in your hand and set it aside, out of play, as a hollow. [action][action]: Discard Warped Reality.

name: Touch of the Beyond
text: Revelation - Place 1 doom on the asset you control with the highest printed resource cost. If no doom was placed by this effect, Touch of the Beyond gains surge.

name: Compulsion
text: Revelation - Put Compulsion into play in your threat area. Forced - When your turn begins: Reveal a random chaos token from the chaos bag. If it is a [skull], [cultist], [tablet], or [elder_thing] token, the first action you perform during this turn must be the below ability, if able. [action][action]: Discard Compulsion.

name: Distorted Reasoning
text: Revelation - Put Distorted Reasoning into play in your threat area. You cannot perform the same type of action twice in a row. (Types include activate, engage, evade, fight, investigate, move, play, resource, and draw.) Forced - At the end of your turn: Test [willpower] (3). If you succeed, discard Distorted Reasoning.

name: Rambling Route
text: Increase the difficulty to fight or evade each concealed mini-card at Rambling Route by 2. [action] Spend X resources: Choose a concealed mini-card adjacent to a location X connections away. Look at its revealed side (without exposing it) . (Group limit once per game.)

name: Rambling Route
text: Increase the difficulty to fight or evade each concealed mini-card at Rambling Route by 2. [action] Spend X resources: Choose a concealed mini-card adjacent to a location X connections away. Look at its revealed side (without exposing it) . (Group limit once per game.)

name: Rambling Route
text: Increase the difficulty to fight or evade each concealed mini-card at Rambling Route by 2. [action] Spend X resources: Choose a concealed mini-card adjacent to a location X connections away. Look at its revealed side (without exposing it) . (Group limit once per game.)

name: Alien Frontier
text: Forced - When you move from Alien Frontier to a connecting location to its left or right, test [agility] (5). If you fail, take 2 damage. [action] Take 1 horror: Look at the revealed side of either a location in the shadows, or the top card of the Otherworld deck. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Alien Frontier
text: Forced - When you move from Alien Frontier to a connecting location above or below it, test [agility] (5). If you fail, take 2 damage. [action] Take 1 horror: Look at the revealed side of either a location in the shadows, or the top card of the Otherworld deck. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Weald of Effigies
text: Forced - After you reveal Weald of Effigies: Look at the top card of your deck. If it is not a weakness, set it aside, out of play, as a hollow. [fast] If there are no clues on Weald of Effigies: Either swap the positions of 2 concealed mini-cards adjacent to locations, or swap the positions of 2 locations in the shadows. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Weald of Effigies
text: Forced - After you reveal Weald of Effigies: Look at the top card of your deck. If it is not a weakness, set it aside, out of play, as a hollow. [fast] If there are no clues on Weald of Effigies: Either swap the positions of 2 concealed mini-cards adjacent to locations, or swap the positions of 2 locations in the shadows. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Cliffs of Insanity
text: Forced - After you reveal Cliffs of Insanity: Move each concealed mini-card adjacent to it so they are arranged as evenly as possible in empty spots adjacent to locations not named Cliffs of Insanity.

name: Paradimensional Terror
text: Peril. Revelation - You must decide (choose one): - Flip each decoy over. Shuffle all concealed mini-cards in play, then place them facedown at random in the positions they were in. - Each investigator takes 1 damage and 1 horror.

name: The Red-Gloved Man
text: Retaliate. Forced - After you flip a key from its [[Unstable]] side to its [[Stable]] side: The Red-Gloved Man attacks you. Then, the Red-Gloved Man shifts each of his attached keys.

name: La Chica Roja
text: Aloof. Spawn - Location farthest from you. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if La Chica Roja is unengaged: Move 1 resource from each investigator to her. She shifts each of her attached keys. [reaction] After La Chica Roja is defeated: Distribute her resources among the investigators, as you wish.

name: The Sanguine Watcher
text: Hunter. Forced - When you defeat The Sanguine Watcher: He shifts each of his attached keys twice.

name: The Beast in a Cowl of Crimson
text: Hunter. Spawn - Location farthest from you. Forced - After you fail a skill test while at The Beast in a Cowl of Crimson's location, if it is ready: It shifts each of its attached keys.

name: The Claret Knight
text: Spawn - Location farthest from you. Each other [[Coterie]] enemy gets +2 fight. Forced - After another [[Coterie]] enemy is discarded from play: The Claret Knight shifts each of his attached keys.

name: Thorne
text: Hunter. Forced - After Thorne enters play: They shift each of their attached keys. [fast] Choose a key attached to your investigator and remove it from the game: Add Thorne to the victory display. Thorne is now the bearer of the chosen key. (Update the Campaign Log.)

name: Desiderio Delgado Álvarez
text: Patrol (nearest location with at least one other [[Coterie]] enemy). Forced - When another [[Coterie]] enemy at Desiderio Delgado Álvarez's location would take 1 or more damage: Deal that damage to him, instead. Then, he shifts each of his attached keys.

name: Amaranth
text: Hunter. Forced - When Amaranth attacks: She shifts each of her attached keys. If damage and/or horror from this attack is assigned to an asset, defeat that asset.

name: Tzu San Niang
text: Hunter. Retaliate. Forced - When you succeed at a skill test by 2 or more while fighting or attempting to evade Tzu San Niang: She shifts each of her attached keys.

name: Aliki Zoni Uperetria
text: Aloof. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Each investigator looks at the top card of their deck. Set each non-weakness card looked at in this way aside, out of play, as a hollow. If there is already a copy of that card set aside as a hollow, Aliki Zoni Uperetria shifts each of her attached keys.

name: Inspector Flint
text: [reaction] When you expose a decoy, exhaust Inspector Flint: Cancel the exposing of that decoy, flip it facedown and either draw 1 card or choose a different concealed mini-card at that location to expose.

name: Agent Ari Quinn
text: While there is a concealed mini-card at your location, you get +1 [intellect], +1 [combat], and +1 [agility]. [reaction] After you expose a card in the shadows, exhaust Agent Ari Quinn: Draw 1 card.

name: Dr. Dewi Irawan
text: You get +1 [willpower]. [fast] Exhaust Dr. Dewi Irawan: Either reveal a card from your hand to shuffle each copy of that card that is set-aside as a hollow into its owner's deck, or discard a card from your hand to shuffle any set-aside hollow into its owner's deck. In either case, gain 1 resource for each card shuffled in this way.

name: Expedited Ticket
text: Permanent. While embarking to a new destination on the map, you may exile Expedited Ticket and mark 1 time in your Campaign Log to travel anywhere on the map.

name: Foundation Intel
text: Foundation Intel cannot leave your hand.

name: Paradimensional Understanding
text: Revelation - You must either (choose one): - Immediately shift an [[Unstable]] key you control. - Flip each [[Stable]] key you control to its [[Unstable]] side.

name: The Mirroring Blade
text: Shift - Deal 2 damage to an enemy at your location, or 1 damage to all enemies at your location. Flip this key to its [[Unstable]] side.

name: The Mirroring Blade
text: Shift - Each investigator takes 1 direct damage. Then, if an investigator shifted this key, flip it to its [[Stable]] side.

name: The Bale Engine
text: Shift - Add 1 ammo, charge, secret, supply, or evidence to an asset controlled by an investigator at your location. Flip this key to its [[Unstable]] side.

name: The Bale Engine
text: Shift - Each investigator loses 3 resources. Then, if an investigator shifted this key, flip it to its [[Stable]] side.

name: The Ruinous Chime
text: Shift - Automatically evade all enemies at your location. Flip this key to its [[Unstable]] side.

name: The Ruinous Chime
text: Shift - Each investigator takes 1 direct horror. Then, if an investigator shifted this key, flip it to its [[Stable]] side.

name: Carson Sinclair
text: You may take an additional action during your turn, which can only be used on the below [action] ability. [action]: Choose another investigator at your location. They immediately take an action as if it were their turn. (Limit once per round for each investigator.) [elder_sign] effect: +0. Draw 1 card. You may resolve this effect anytime another investigator at your location resolves their [elder_sign] effect.

name: "As you wish"
text: Carson Sinclair deck only. Commit only to a skill test being performed by another investigator. If this test succeeds, the performing investigator draws 1 card. If this test fails, you draw 1 card.

name: Selfless to a Fault
text: Revelation - Put Selfless to a Fault into play in your threat area. Forced - At the end of your turn, if you did not commit at least 1 card to a skill test performed by another investigator this turn: Take 1 horror and shuffle Selfless to a Fault into your deck.

name: Vincent Lee
text: [reaction] After one of your card effects heals damage from an investigator or an investigator's [[Ally]] asset: Add one of your set-aside On the Mend skills to that investigator's hand. [elder_sign] effect: +1. You may heal 1 damage from an investigator or [[Ally]] asset at your location.

name: Bonesaw
text: Vincent Lee deck only. [action]: Fight. You get +2 [combat] for this attack. If this attack is successful, you may take 1 damage to deal +1 damage for this attack. [action]: Heal 5 damage from an investigator at your location. Test [intellect] (4). If you fail, that investigator suffers 1 physical trauma. (Max once per game.)

name: On the Mend
text: Limit 1 On the Mend in your hand. Commit only to a test you are performing. Forced - When On the Mend would be discarded from anywhere: Set it aside, out of play, instead.

name: Wounded Bystander
text: Revelation - Put Wounded Bystander into play in your play area with 3 damage on it. It cannot leave play while it has damage (unless it is defeated). If it has no damage, discard it. Forced - When you take non-direct damage: At least 1 of it must be assigned to Wounded Bystander, if able. Forced - If Wounded Bystander is defeated: You suffer 1 mental trauma.

name: Kymani Jones
text: [fast]: Engage an exhausted enemy at your location. [reaction] When you attempt to evade an exhausted non-[[Elite]] enemy: Add your [intellect] to your skill value for this attempt. If you succeed by at least X, discard that enemy. X is that enemy's remaining health. [elder_sign] effect: +1. If there is an exhausted enemy at your location, you automatically succeed instead.

name: Grappling Hook
text: Kymani Jones deck only. Actions performed using Grappling Hook do not provoke attacks of opportunity. [action][action] Exhaust Grappling Hook: Take up to 3 different basic actions from the following list, in any order (engage, evade, investigate, or move). If you investigate, use your [agility] instead of your [intellect].

name: Agent Fletcher
text: Alert. Hunter. Prey - Kymani Jones only. While Kymani Jones is evading Agent Fletcher, reduce their [intellect] value to 0.

name: Amina Zidane
text: [reaction] When you play an asset: Reduce its resource cost by 3. It enters play with 1 doom on it. (Limit once per round.) [elder_sign] effect: +2. You may move all doom from a card at your location to another card at your location.

name: Word of Woe
text: Amina Zidane deck only. Fast. Play only during your turn. Place 1 doom on an asset you control to resolve an [action] ability on it, ignoring all costs (including its [action] cost) . Then, if Word of Weal is in your discard pile, you may shuffle it into your deck.

name: Word of Weal
text: Amina Zidane deck only. Fast. Play when you perform a skill test on an asset with 1 or more doom on it. Add your [willpower] to your skill value for this test. Then, if Word of Woe is in your discard pile, you may shuffle it into your deck.

name: Deafening Silence
text: Revelation - Move 1 doom from an asset you control to the current agenda. This may cause the current agenda to advance. If no doom is moved in this way, shuffle Deafening Silence back into your deck.

name: Darrell Simmons
text: You begin the game with Darrell's Kodak in play. [fast] During a skill test at your location, spend 1 evidence from an asset you control: Reduce the difficulty of this test by 2. (Limit once per test.) [elder_sign] effect: +1. Place 1 evidence on an asset you control.

name: Darrell's Kodak
text: Darrell Simmons deck only. [reaction] After an enemy or treachery enters play, exhaust Darrell's Kodak: Place 1 resource (from the token pool) on that enemy or treachery, as evidence. [reaction] After you discover any number of clues: Move that many evidence on enemies or treacheries at that location (or not at any location) to Darrell's Kodak.

name: Ruined Film
text: Revelation - Remove 4 evidence from cards you control. For each evidence you cannot remove in this way, take 1 horror.

name: Charlie Kane
text: You have 3 additional ally slots. [fast] During a skill test you are performing, exhaust an [[Ally]] asset you control: For this test, you get +1 skill value, plus an additional +1 for each skill icon that [[Ally]] asset has that matches this test's type. [elder_sign] effect: +3. Ready an [[Ally]] asset at your location.

name: Bonnie Walsh
text: Charlie Kane deck only. [reaction] After you exhaust Bonnie Walsh: Ready another [[Ally]] asset you control. (Limit once per round.)

name: Burden of Leadership
text: Revelation - If you control no [[Ally]] assets, shuffle Burden of Leadership back into your deck. Otherwise, for each [[Ally]] asset you control, you must either exhaust it or deal it 1 direct damage and 1 direct horror.

name: Hunter's Armor
text: Customizable.

name: Runic Axe
text: Customizable. Uses (4 charges). Replenish 1 of these charges at the start of each round. [action]: Fight. You get +1 [combat] for this attack. Before this attack, you may spend any number of charges to imbue the axe with that many different inscriptions - - Accuracy - You get an additional +2 [combat] for this attack. - Power - This attack deals +1 damage.

name: Custom Modifications
text: Customizable. Attach to a [[Firearm]] asset you control. Limit 1 per asset. [reaction] When you reveal a non-[auto_fail] chaos token attacking with attached asset, exhaust Custom Modifications: Cancel that chaos token, return it to the chaos bag, and reveal a new one.

name: Obsidian Bracelet
text: Obsidian Bracelet may only be assigned damage and/or horror dealt by treachery effects. Obsidian Bracelet may be assigned damage and/or horror dealt to other investigators at your location.

name: Bolas
text: Evade. Attempt to evade using [combat] instead of [agility]. If you succeed and the enemy is non-[[Elite]], attach Bolas to it. Attached enemy gets -1 evade value. Forced - After attached enemy moves: Exhaust it.

name: Breach the Door
text: Test [combat] (1). If you succeed, attach Breach the Door to your location with 1 resource on it for each point you succeeded by, as leads. Reduce attached location's shroud by 1 for each lead on Breach the Door.

name: Grievous Wound
text: Fast. Play after you successfully attack a non-[[Elite]] enemy using a [[Melee]] asset. Attach Grievous Wound to the attacked enemy. Forced - At the end of the round: Deal 1 damage to attached enemy.

name: Motivational Speech
text: Parley. Choose an investigator at your location. That investigator may play an [[Ally]] asset from their hand, reducing its cost by 3.

name: One in the Chamber
text: Fast. Play at the end of an attack or effect that spends the last ammo from a [[Firearm]] asset you control. Add 1 ammo to that asset. You get +3 skill value for your next attack with that asset this round.

name: Fighting Lessons
text: Commit only to a skill test during an attack or evasion attempt. You can commit this skill to an investigator's test at any location.

name: Helping Hand
text: You may commit Helping Hand to any type of test. Max 1 committed per test. While Helping Hand is committed to a skill test, double the skill icons of each other card committed to that test.

name: Bestow Resolve
text: Uses (4 charges). [fast] During a skill test performed by an investigator at your location or a connecting location, spend 1 charge: Commit a non-weakness card from your hand to that skill test, treating all of its skill icons as [wild].

name: Field Agent
text: You get +1 [intellect]. [fast] Exhaust Field Agent and deal 1 horror to it: Discover 1 clue at your location.

name: Guard Dog
text: [fast] Exhaust Guard Dog: Engage an enemy at your location. That enemy attacks you. [reaction] When an enemy attack deals damage and/or horror to Guard Dog: Deal 1 damage to the attacking enemy. Card design by the Council-in-Exile at Arkham Nights 2020.

name: Handcuffs
text: Fast. [action] If Handcuffs is not attached to an enemy: Evade. Use only on a [[Humanoid]] enemy. This evasion attempt uses [combat] instead of [agility]. If you succeed, remove all doom from the just-evaded enemy and attach Handcuffs to it. If the attached enemy is non-[[Elite]], it cannot ready and doom cannot be placed on it.

name: Prepared for the Worst
text: Choose an investigator at your location. That investigator search the top 9 cards of their deck for a [[Weapon]] asset, adds it to their hand or plays it (paying its resource cost) , and shuffles their deck.

name: Martyr's Vambrace
text: You get +1 skill value while resolving revelation abilities on encounter cards. [reaction] After another investigator at your location draws a non-peril encounter card, before resolving that card's effects, exhaust Martyr's Vambrace: You are considered to have drawn that encounter card, instead.

name: Girish Kadakia
text: Girish Kadakia may be assigned damage and/or horror dealt to other investigators at your location. [fast] During a skill test being performed by an investigator at your location, exhaust Girish Kadakia: That investigator gets +2 skill value for this test. If this test is successful, heal 1 damage or horror from Girish Kadakia.

name: Ever Vigilant
text: One at a time, investigators at your location, as a group, may play up to 4 assets in total from their hands, reducing the resource cost of each by 1.

name: Alchemical Distillation
text: Customizable. Uses (3 supplies). [action] Spend 1 supply: Choose an investigator at your location and test [intellect] (1). If you succeed, that investigator performs one of the following options - - Draw 2 cards. - Gain 2 resources.

name: Empirical Hypothesis
text: Customizable. Limit 1 per investigator. Forced - At the start of the round: Choose one of the following criteria for this round - You fail a test by 2 or more. - You succeed at a test by 3 or more. When the chosen criteria is met, you may exhaust Empirical Hypothesis to add 1 evidence to it. [fast] Spend 1 evidence: Draw 1 card.

name: The Raven Quill
text: Customizable. When you purchase The Raven Quill, name a [[Tome]] or [[Spell]] asset and record that name on its upgrade sheet. Attach to a named asset you control. [reaction] When you resign or the game ends: Either mark a checkbox on The Raven Quill's upgrade sheet, or reduce the experience cost to upgrade the attached asset before the next scenario by 1.

name: Dissection Tools
text: [reaction] After an enemy at your location is defeated: Place 1 resource (from the token pool) on this card, as evidence. While Dissection Tools has... - ... 1 or more evidence, you get +1 [agility]. - ... 2 or more evidence, you get +1 [combat]. - ... 3 or more evidence, you get +1 sanity.

name: Grim Memoir
text: Uses (4 secrets). [action] Spend 1 secret: Investigate. You get +2 [intellect] for this investigation. If you succeed by 2 or more, you may draw 1 card.

name: Research Notes
text: [reaction] After a player card ability places 1 or more of your clues on your location: Place that many resources on Research Notes, as evidence. [action]: Test [intellect] (0). For each point you succeed by, you may spend 1 evidence to discover 1 clue at your location.

name: Bizarre Diagnosis
text: Place 1 of your clues on your location. Then, heal 3 damage from an investigator or [[Ally]] asset at your location.

name: Captivating Discovery
text: Search the top 6 cards of your deck. You may place up to 3 of your clues on your location. For each clue placed in this way, choose and add 2 of the searched cards to your hand. Shuffle your deck.

name: Map the Area
text: Investigate. Add your [willpower] or [agility] to your skill value for this investigation. If you succeed, instead of discovering clues at this location, attach Map the Area to it. Limit 1 per location. Reduce the difficulty of all skill tests at attached location by 1.

name: Analysis
text: After revealing a chaos token for this skill test, you may place 1 of your clues on your location to cancel that chaos token, return it to the chaos bag, and reveal a new one. (You may do this any number of times.)

name: Lab Coat
text: [reaction] When you would fail a skill test on a [seeker] card by 1 or less, exhaust Lab Coat: You succeed by 0, instead.

name: Orphic Theory
text: Uses (4 secrets). [fast] Spend 1 secret: Choose any non-weakness treachery not attached to an [[Elite]] enemy. Until the end of the round, treat that card's printed text box as if it were blank (except for [[Traits]]).

name: Existential Riddle
text: Parley. Choose a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location and test [willpower] or [intellect] (8). This test gets -1 difficulty for each other card in your hand. If you succeed, automatically evade the chosen enemy and attach Existential Riddle to it. Attached enemy gains aloof.

name: Guidance
text: Choose another investigator at your location who has yet to take their turn this round. During their turn this round, the chosen investigator gets +1 to each of their skills, and may take an additional action. Playing Guidance does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

name: Dr. William T. Maleson
text: [reaction] When any investigator draws an encounter card from the encounter deck, exhaust Dr. William T. Maleson and place 1 of your clues on your location: That investigator draws another card from the top of the encounter deck, chooses one of those 2 cards to resolve, and cancels and discards the other.

name: Press Pass
text: [reaction] After you spend 1 or more clues or place 1 or more clues on your location, exhaust Press Pass: You may take an additional action during this turn (or your next turn if it is not currently your turn).

name: Surgical Kit
text: Uses (4 supplies). [reaction] When one of your card effects heals damage from an investigator, spend 1 supply: Either that effect heals 1 additional damage, or you draw 1 card and heal 1 horror. [action] [action]: Heal 3 damage from an investigator or [[Ally]] asset at your location.

name: Fingerprint Kit
text: Uses (3 supplies). [action] Exhaust Fingerprint Kit and spend 1 supply: Investigate. You get +2 [intellect] for this investigation. If you succeed, you discover 2 additional clues at your location.

name: Gray's Anatomy
text: [action]: Choose a card at your location and test [intellect] (1). For each point you succeed by, choose one - - The next time that card would be healed this round, heal +1 damage/horror (to a maximum of +3). - The next time that card would be dealt damage this round, deal +1 damage (to a maximum of +3).

name: Damning Testimony
text: Customizable. Uses (3 evidence). [action] Exhaust Damning Testimony and choose an enemy at any location: Investigate (your location) . If you succeed, you may spend 1 evidence to discover 1 additional clue at the chosen enemy's location.

name: Friends in Low Places
text: Customizable. When you purchase Friends in Low Places, choose and record a [[Trait]] on its upgrade sheet. Look at the top 6 cards of your deck. For each looked-at card with the chosen [[Trait]](s), you may spend 1 resource to add that card to your hand. Shuffle the remaining cards into your deck.

name: Honed Instinct
text: Customizable. Fast. Play this card only after one of the following conditions is met: - The agenda or act advances. - You succeed at a skill test by 3 or more. Immediately take an action as if it were your turn. (Max once per round.)

name: Disguise
text: Uses (4 supplies). [action] Spend 1 supply: Evade. You get +2 [agility] for this evasion attempt. If you succeed and the evaded enemy is non-[[Elite]], it does not ready during the next upkeep phase.

name: Embezzled Treasure
text: [fast] Exhaust Embezzled Treasure: Move up to 2 resources from your resource pool to Embezzled Treasure (to a maximum of 10 resources on it). Forced - When you resign or the game ends: For every 2 resources on Embezzled Treasure, an investigator of your choice begins the next scenario of the campaign with 1 additional resource in their resource pool.

name: Thieves' Kit
text: Uses (6 supplies). [action] Spend 1 supply: Investigate. You may use [agility] instead of [intellect] for this investigation. If you succeed, gain 1 resource.

name: Hidden Pocket
text: Fast. Play only during your turn. Attach to a [[Clothing]] or [[Armor]] asset you control. Attached asset gains: "You have 1 additional hand or accessory slot (your choice), which can only be used to hold an [[Illicit]] asset."

name: Hit and Run
text: Fast. Play only during your turn. Put an [[Ally]] asset from your hand into play. At the end of your turn, if that asset is still in play, return it to your hand.

name: "I'll take that!"
text: Fast. Play when you successfully evade a [[Humanoid]] enemy or successfully investigate. Play an [[Item]] asset from your hand, reducing its cost by X, where X is the amount you succeeded by. Attach "I'll take that!" to that asset. Attached asset gains [[Illicit]].

name: Kicking the Hornet's Nest
text: Search the top 9 cards of the encounter deck for a non-[[Elite]] enemy and spawn it engaged with you (instead of its normal spawn location) . Then, discover 1 clue at your location and gain X resources, where X is that enemy's health value. Shuffle the encounter deck.

name: Quick Getaway
text: Fast. Play when an enemy attacks you. Evade. Attempt to evade the attacking enemy. If you succeed, cancel that enemy's attack.

name: Calculated Risk
text: Max 1 committed per skill test. Commit only to a test you are performing of any type, and only if it is your turn. Calculated Risk gains [wild] for each action you performed this turn (including this one) . After this test ends, end your turn.

name: Stylish Coat
text: [reaction] When you gain 1 or more resources during your turn via another player card effect, exhaust Stylish Coat: Gain 1 additional resource.

name: Chuck Fergus
text: [reaction] When you play a [[Tactic]] or [[Trick]] event, exhaust Chuck Fergus: Choose one - - That event gains fast. - That event costs 2 fewer resources to play. - You get +2 skill value while performing a skill test during the resolution of that event.

name: Dirty Fighting
text: Limit 1 per investigator. While attacking, parleying, or attempting to evade an exhausted enemy, you get +2 skill value. [reaction] After you evade an enemy, exhaust Dirty Fighting: Take a fight action against that enemy. Ignore the aloof keyword for this attack.

name: Breaking and Entering
text: Investigate. Add your [agility] value to your skill value for this investigation. If you succeed by 1 or more, you may automatically evade an enemy at this location. If you succeed by 3 or more, return Breaking and Entering to your hand at the end of your turn. This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

name: Thieves' Kit
text: Uses (6 supplies). [action] Spend 1 supply: Investigate. For this investigation, you may use [agility] instead of [intellect], and you get +1 skill value. If you succeed, gain 1 resource (2 resources instead if you succeed by 2 or more).

name: Trigger Man
text: [reaction] After Trigger Man enters play: Attach an [[Illicit]] asset from your hand to Trigger Man (it is considered to be in play and under your control) . [fast] Exhaust Trigger Man and spend 1 resource: Resolve an [action] ability on attached asset, without paying its [action] cost. Resolve that ability with a base skill value of 4.

name: Underworld Market
text: Permanent. Exceptional. You get +10 deck size. [reaction] Before drawing your opening hand, choose 10 [[Illicit]] cards from your deck: Shuffle them together and place them next to Underworld Market, as your market deck. [reaction] At the start of your turn: Reveal the top 2 cards of your market deck. You may spend 1 resource to draw 1 of them. Place the rest on the bottom, in any order.

name: Clean Sneak
text: Fast. Play only during your turn. For each exhausted non-swarm enemy at your location, choose a different option: - Gain 2 resources. - Deal 2 damage to that enemy. - Discover 1 clue at your location. - Draw 1 card.

name: Living Ink
text: Customizable. When you purchase Living Ink, choose a skill and circle it on its upgrade sheet. Uses (3 charges). Remove 1 charge from Living Ink at the start of each of your turns. If Living Ink has no charges, discard it. You get +1 to the chosen skill(s).

name: Summoned Servitor
text: Customizable. As an additional cost to play Summoned Servitor, you must discard another asset you control. Summoned Servitor enters play at your location. It can take 1 action during each of your turns, which can only be used to take the following action: - Move Summoned Servitor to a connecting revealed location.

name: Power Word
text: Customizable. Fast. Play only during your turn. Attach to a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location. [action] If attached enemy is at your location: Parley. Give it a command (limit once per round per command) - - "Go." This enemy moves once in a direction of your choice. - "Cower." This enemy exhausts.

name: Ceremonial Sickle
text: [action]: Fight. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [combat] for this attack. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Ceremonial Sickle and place 1 doom on it to get +1 skill value and deal +1 damage for this attack. - If this attack defeats an enemy, remove 1 doom from Ceremonial Sickle.

name: Dowsing Rod
text: [action]: Investigate. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [intellect] for this investigation. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Dowsing Rod and place 1 doom on it to move to a connecting location. - If this investigation discovers the last clue at a location, remove 1 doom from Dowsing Rod.

name: Hallowed Chalice
text: [action]: Choose an investigator at your location and either (choose one) - - Exhaust Hallowed Chalice and place 1 doom on it to heal 2 damage or 2 horror from that investigator. - Heal 1 damage or 1 horror from that investigator. If you heal the last damage or horror from that investigator, remove 1 doom from Hallowed Chalice.

name: Onyx Pentacle
text: [action]: Evade. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [agility] for this evasion attempt. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Onyx Pentacle and place 1 doom on it to target any enemy at your location or a connecting location, and get +1 skill value for this evasion attempt. - If you succeed by 2 or more, remove 1 doom from Onyx Pentacle.

name: Eldritch Initiation
text: For each arcane slot you have, draw 1 card (max 5). For each filled arcane slot you have, choose and discard 1 card from your hand.

name: Explosive Ward
text: Before you play Explosive Ward, you may discard any number of cards from your arcane slots. Deal X damage to a non-[[Elite]] enemy engaged with you. X cannot be greater than the number of empty arcane slots you have. This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

name: String of Curses
text: Parley. Choose a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location and either (choose one): - Automatically evade that enemy and place 1 doom on it. It cannot take damage for the remainder of the round. Discover 1 clue at your location. - If that enemy has 1 or more doom on it, defeat it. Gain 1 resource for each doom that was on it.

name: Binder's Jar
text: You get +1 arcane slot for each enemy beneath Binder's Jar. [reaction] After a non-[[Elite]] enemy is defeated at your location: Place that enemy facedown beneath Binder's Jar (limit 2 enemies beneath it). [reaction] When an enemy attacks you, discard an enemy beneath Binder's Jar that shares a [[Trait]] with the attacking enemy: Cancel that attack.

name: Ghastly Possession
text: After you commit Ghastly Possession to a skill test on an asset, choose one: - Place 1 doom on that asset. Ghastly Possession gains [wild][wild]. - If this test is successful, either remove 1 doom from this asset, or replenish half of its uses (rounded down).

name: Astral Mirror
text: For each of your arcane slots that are empty, you have one additional hand slot. You may take an additional action during your turn, which can only be used to play an asset into one of your hand slots.

name: Elle Rubash
text: You get +1 skill value while resolving a skill test on an attached asset. 1 doom on each attached asset does not count towards the agenda's doom threshold. [fast] Exhaust Elle Rubash: Choose an asset in your play area with 1 or more doom on it. Attach it to Elle Rubash, or switch it with an attached asset. (Limit 2 attached assets).

name: Moonlight Ritual
text: Fast. Play only during your turn. Remove all doom from a non-[[Elite]] card at your location.

name: Sin-Eater
text: Permanent. Exceptional. [fast] Exhaust Sin-Eater: Move 1 doom from an asset you control to Sin-Eater. Then, either ready that asset or place 1 charge on it. [action]: Remove all doom from Sin-Eater.

name: Uncage the Soul
text: You may discard a [[Spell]] or [[Ritual]] asset you control. Play a [[Spell]] or [[Ritual]] card from your hand or discard pile, reducing its resource cost by 3.

name: Ceremonial Sickle
text: [action]: Fight. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [combat] for this attack. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Ceremonial Sickle and place 1 doom on it to get +3 skill value and deal +1 damage for this attack. - If this attack defeats an enemy, ready Ceremonial Sickle and remove all doom from it.

name: Dowsing Rod
text: [action]: Investigate. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [intellect] for this investigation. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Dowsing Rod and place 1 doom on it to move to a connecting location and get +2 skill value for this investigation. - If this investigation discovers the last clue at a location, ready Dowsing Rod and remove all doom from it.

name: Onyx Pentacle
text: [action]: Evade. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [agility] for this evasion attempt. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Onyx Pentacle and place 1 doom on it to target any enemy at your location or a connecting location, and get +3 skill value for this evasion attempt. - If you succeed by 2 or more, ready Onyx Pentacle and remove all doom from it.

name: Pocket Multi Tool
text: Customizable. Limit 1 per investigator. [fast] During a skill test you are performing, exhaust Pocket Multi Tool: You get +1 skill value for this test.

name: Makeshift Trap
text: Customizable. Uses (2 time). If Makeshift Trap has no time, discard it. Attach to your location. Each non-[[Elite]] enemy at attached location get -1 fight and -1 evade. Forced - At the end of the round: Remove 1 time from Makeshift Trap.

name: Grizzled
text: Customizable. When you purchase Grizzled, choose and record two [[Traits]] on its upgrade sheet. If this is a skill test on or against an encounter card (including fighting, evading, or parleying) , Grizzled gains [wild][wild] for each of the chosen traits that encounter card possesses.

name: Idol of Xanatos
text: [reaction] When you are dealt damage and/or horror, exhaust Idol of Xanatos and discard up to 3 cards from your hand: Cancel that much damage/horror.

name: Improvised Shield
text: Improvised Shield can only be played from your discard pile. Forced - When Improvised Shield is defeated: Shuffle it into your deck instead of discarding it.

name: End of the Road
text: Fast. Play during your turn only if the final agenda is in play. Draw 1 card, gain 1 resource, and gain 1 additional action. Remove End of the Road from the game.

name: Exploit Weakness
text: Fast. Play before revealing a chaos token during an attack or evasion attempt you are performing, and only if the difficulty of this test is currently 0. This test automatically succeeds. Instead of this test's normal effects, discard the attacked or evaded enemy (if it is [[Elite]], automatically evade it and deal it 3 damage instead).

name: Making Preparations
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - Choose two skills ([willpower], [intellect], [combat], or [agility]). Until the end of the investigation phase, each investigator gets +1 to each of the chosen skills, and -1 to each other skill.

name: Predator or Prey
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - If there are no enemies in play, draw 1 card. Otherwise, you must decide (choose one): - Each unengaged non-[[Elite]] enemy moves once toward the nearest investigator. - Each investigator disengages from each enemy engaged with them and moves once away from the nearest enemy.

name: Shed a Light
text: Fast. Play before revealing a chaos token during an investigation you are performing, and only if the difficulty of this test is currently 0. This test automatically succeeds. You discover 1 additional clue at this location and 1 additional clue at any location.

name: At a Crossroads
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - Choose an investigator and decide (choose one): - The chosen investigator must immediately take an action as if it were their turn, then discards 1 card at random from their hand. - The chosen investigator loses 1 action during their next turn, then draws 3 cards.

name: Lifeline
text: Max 1 per turn. Fast. Play when an investigator's turn would end. That investigator may take X additional actions during their turn. X is the number of skill tests they failed during their turn. Exile Lifeline.

name: Nature of the Beast
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - Reveal the top 3 cards of the encounter deck. Choose an investigator to draw 1 of the revealed cards and discard the rest. Then, choose an investigator to discover 1 clue at any revealed location.

name: Gumption
text: Max 1 committed per skill test. You may commit Gumption to any type of skill test. While Gumption is committed to a skill test, that test gets -2 difficulty.

name: Baseball Bat
text: [action]: Fight. You get +2 [combat] for this attack. This attack deals +1 damage. If a [skull] or [auto_fail] symbol is revealed during this attack, either (choose one): - Return Baseball Bat to your hand after this attack. - This attack deals an additional +1 damage. Discard Baseball Bat after this attack.

name: Katja Eastbank
text: [reaction] When you draw a non-weakness card from the top of your deck, before resolving any of its effects, exhaust Katja Eastbank: Place that card facedown beneath her (limit 5 cards beneath her). Draw a new card to replace it. [action]: Draw any card beneath Katja Eastbank.

name: Heed the Dream
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - Reveal the top card from each investigator's deck and place them in a pile. Repeat this two more times. Choose one of the three piles. Each investigator draws each of their cards from that pile. Shuffle each weakness in each other pile into its owner's deck. Remove each other revealed card from the game.

name: Salvage
text: Choose an [[Item]] asset in your discard pile. Either (choose one): - Remove that asset from the game and gain resources equal to its cost. - Play that asset (paying its cost) .

name: Old Keyring
text: Uses (3 keys). If there are no keys on Old Keyring, discard it. [action]: Investigate. Your location gets -2 shroud for this investigation. If you succeed, remove 1 key from Old Keyring, and if this test's difficulty is 0, you discover 1 additional clue.

name: Fickle Fortune
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - You must decide (choose one): - Place 1 doom on the current agenda. Each investigator heals 3 damage and 3 horror. - Remove 1 doom from the current agenda. Each investigator takes 1 direct damage and 1 direct horror. Remove Fickle Fortune from the game.

name: Hyperphysical Shotcaster
text: Customizable. Uses (4 aether). If Hyperphysical Shotcaster has no aether, discard it. [action] Spend 1 aether: Resolve the Manifest ability of the Hyperphysical Shotcaster's current form. [fast] Exhaust Hyperphysical Shotcaster: Change its form (to one you have unlocked on its upgrade sheet).

name: Tool Belt
text: Each attached asset takes up no slots and its text box is treated as if it were blank (except for [[Traits]]). [fast] Exhaust Tool Belt: Choose one - attach a [[Tool]] asset in your play area to Tool Belt, switch a [[Tool]] asset in your play area with an attached asset, or detach an attached asset.

name: Refine
text: As an additional cost to play Refine, spend an action. Immediately mark a checkbox on an upgrade sheet for a customizable card you own, even if it is not in play. Max once per game for each investigator.

name: Flashlight
text: Uses (4 supplies). [reaction] When you perform a skill test while investigating or attempting to evade, spend 1 supply: This test gets -2 difficulty. Card design by the Council-in-Exile at Arkham Nights 2020.

name: Soul Sanctification
text: Permanent. Exceptional. For every point of damage and/or horror you heal in excess of an investigator's current damage/horror, place 1 resource on Soul Sanctification, as an offering. This still counts as healing. [fast] Spend 1 offering: You get +2 skill value for this test. (Limit twice per test.)

name: Lurker in the Dark
text: [guardian] investigator only. Hunter. Spawn - Any connecting location. Lurker in the Dark can only be attacked or damaged using [[Weapon]] assets or [[Tactic]] events, and takes 1 fewer damage from each source.

name: Quantum Paradox
text: [seeker] investigator only. As an additional cost to play Quantum Paradox, you must choose and discard 4 other cards from your hand. Forced - If Quantum Paradox is in your hand at the end of your turn: Reveal it and take 1 horror.

name: Pay Your Due
text: [rogue] investigator only. As an additional cost to play Pay Your Due, you may spend any number of additional actions. Reduce its cost by 5 for each additional action spent. Forced - If Pay Your Due is in your hand at the end of your turn: Reveal it and take 1 damage.

name: Ectoplasmic Horror
text: [mystic] investigator only. Hunter. The first time you reveal a chaos token while attacking or attempting to evade Ectoplasmic Horror, reveal an additional token for each empty arcane slot you have.

name: Underprepared
text: [survivor] investigator only. Play only if Underprepared is the only card in your hand and you have exactly 1 resource in your resource pool. While Underprepared is in your hand, each card you commit to skill tests is considered to have 1 fewer matching skill icon.

name: .45 Thompson
text: Uses (5 ammo). Ammo spent from .45 Thompson is placed in your resource pool, as resources. [action] Spend 1 ammo: Fight. You get +2 [combat] and deal +1 damage for this attack.

name: .45 Thompson
text: Uses (5 ammo). [action] Spend 1 ammo: Fight. You get +2 [combat] and deal +1 damage for this attack. If you succeed by at least X, you may spend 1 ammo to deal this attack's damage to another enemy at your location, where X is that enemy's fight value.

name: Scroll of Secrets
text: Uses (3 secrets). [action] Exhaust Scroll of Secrets and spend 1 secret: Look at the bottom 3 cards of any investigator's deck or the encounter deck. You may discard 1 of those cards. You may add 1 of those cards to its owner's hand. Place the rest of those cards on either the top or bottom of their deck, in any order.

name: Scroll of Secrets
text: Uses (4 secrets). [action] Exhaust Scroll of Secrets and spend 1 secret: Look at the top or bottom card of any investigator's deck or the encounter deck. Then, either discard that card, add it to its owner's hand, place it on the bottom of its deck, or place it on top of its deck.

name: Tennessee Sour Mash
text: Fast. Uses (2 supplies). [fast] Exhaust Tennessee Sour Mash and spend 1 supply: You get +3 [willpower] for a skill test on a treachery card. [action] Discard Tennessee Sour Mash: Fight. You get +3 [combat] and deal +1 damage for this attack.

name: Tennessee Sour Mash
text: Uses (3 supplies). [fast] Exhaust Tennessee Sour Mash and spend 1 supply: You get +2 [willpower] for a skill test on a treachery card. [action] Discard Tennessee Sour Mash: Fight. You get +3 [combat] for this attack. If this attack succeeds against a non-[[Elite]] enemy, automatically evade it.

name: Enchanted Blade
text: Uses (3 charges). [action]: Fight. You get +2 [combat] for this attack. If you succeed, you may spend 1 charge to empower the blade, dealing +1 damage for this attack. If the blade is empowered and this attack defeats an enemy, draw 1 card and heal 1 horror.

name: Enchanted Blade
text: Uses (4 charges). [action]: Fight. You get +2 [combat] for this attack. As an additional cost to use this ability, you may spend up to 2 charges to empower the blade (for each charge spent in this way, you get +1 [combat] and deal +1 damage for this attack).

name: Grisly Totem
text: [reaction] After you commit a card to a skill test, exhaust Grisly Totem: That card gains another instance of one of its skill icons of your choice. If that skill test is successful, the performing investigator draws 1 card.

name: Grisly Totem
text: [reaction] After you commit a card to a skill test, exhaust Grisly Totem: That card gains another instance of one of its skill icons of your choice. If that skill test fails, return that card to your hand.

name: The Council's Coffer
text: Uses (1 [per_investigator] locks). If there are no locks on The Council's Coffer, each investigator searches his or her deck or discard pile for any card and immediately plays it at no cost. Exile The Council's Coffer. (Max once per campaign.) [action] [action]: Test any skill (5). If you succeed, remove 1 lock. Any investigator at your location may activate this ability. Card design by The Council at Arkham Nights 2017.

name: For the Greater Good
text: Easy / Standard [skull]: -X. X is the highest number of doom on a [[Cultist]] enemy in play. [cultist]: -2. Reveal another token. [tablet]: -3. If you fail, place 1 doom on the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy. [elder_thing]: -3. If you fail, move 1 doom from the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy to the current agenda.

name: THE HIEROPHANT · V
text: Forced - When you defeat a [[Silver Twilight]] enemy: You leave behind some evidence of the scuffle. Move 1 doom from each enemy at that location to the current agenda.

name: Ends and Means
text: Each [[Silver Twilight]] enemy in a [[Sanctum]] location loses aloof. Forced - When you defeat a [[Silver Twilight]] enemy: You leave behind some evidence of the scuffle. Move 1 doom from each enemy at that location to the current agenda.

name: Infiltrating the Lodge
text: [reaction] After you evade an enemy: Remove all doom from it.

name: Obtaining the Device
text: Objective - If Nathan Wick is added to the victory display by any means, choose an investigator to take control of the attached Puzzle Box, and advance.

name: The Four Keys
text: Objective - If investigators at the same location control the Puzzle Box, the [skull] key, the [cultist] key, the [tablet] key, and the [elder_thing] key, advance.

name: Lodge Gates
text: Enemies cannot spawn at the Lodge Gates. [action]: Resign. On second thought, maybe coming here was a bad idea.

name: Lodge Gates
text: Enemies cannot spawn at the Lodge Gates. [action]: Resign. On second thought, maybe coming here was a bad idea.

name: Lobby
text: Forced - After the Lobby is revealed: Place 1 random set-aside key on it.

name: Lodge Cellar
text: Forced - After the Lodge Cellar is revealed: Place 1 random set-aside key on it.

name: Lounge
text: Forced - After the Lounge is revealed: Put the set-aside Vault and Library locations into play. Put the set-aside August Lindquist asset into play in the Lounge. Place 1 random set-aside key on him.

name: Vault
text: Forced - After the Vault is revealed: Place 1 random set-aside key on it.

name: Library
text: While you are investigating the Library, if you control the [tablet] key, the Library gets -3 shroud.

name: Lodge Catacombs
text: Forced - When Lodge Catacombs is revealed: Put the set-aside Inner Sanctum and each of the set-aside Sanctum Doorway locations into play.

name: Ceremony Room
text: Forced - When the round ends: Each investigator and each [[Ally]] asset in the Ceremony Room takes 1 direct horror. Cancel this effect if an investigator in the Ceremony Room controls the [skull] key.

name: Holding Cells
text: Forced - After Holding Cells is revealed: Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Cultist]] enemy and spawn it in the Holding Cells. Shuffle the encounter deck.

name: Inner Sanctum
text: Forced - After the Inner Sanctum is revealed: Place 1 random set-aside key on it.

name: Nathan Wick
text: Retaliate. [action]: Parley. Test [willpower] (3) to persuade Nathan to let you find another option. If you succeed, place 1 resource on Nathan Wick. Then, if there are 1 [per_investigator] resources on him, add him to the victory display. If you fail, Nathan Wick attacks you.

name: Nathan Wick
text: Alert. Forced - After you evade Nathan Wick by 3 or more: You steal the puzzle box. Add Nathan Wick to the victory display.

name: Lodge Jailor
text: Spawn - Any [[Sanctum]] location. Aloof. Forced - After Lodge Jailor enters play: Place 2 doom and 1 random set-aside key on it. [action]: Parley. Test [intellect] (3). If you succeed, either remove 1 doom from Lodge Jailor, or take control of its key.

name: Cell Keeper
text: Spawn - Any [[Sanctum]] location. Alert. Forced - After Cell Keeper enters play: Place 2 doom and 1 random set-aside key on it. Forced - When you evade Cell Keeper by 2 or more: Take control of its key.

name: Summoned Beast
text: Retaliate. Hunter. Summoned Beast gets +1 fight, +1 evade, +1 damage, and +1 horror for every 2 doom in play. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Summoned Beast defeats each [[Humanoid]] enemy at its location. Move all doom from each of those enemies to Summoned Beast. Objective - If the Summoned Beast is defeated, (→R3) .

name: Knight of the Inner Circle
text: Spawn - Any location connected to yours. Prey - Most ([skull]/[cultist]/[tablet]/[elder_thing]) keys controlled. Alert. Aloof. Hunter. Forced - After you enter Knight of the Inner Circle's location (or vice-versa): Test [agility] (4). If you fail, Knight of the Inner Circle engages you.

name: Knight of the Outer Void
text: Spawn - Any location connected to yours. Aloof. Peril. Retaliate. Revelation - After Knight of the Outer Void spawns, place either 1 or 2 doom on it. [action]: Parley. Test [willpower] or [intellect] (4). If you succeed, take control of 1 doom on Knight of the Outer Void, and flip it to its clue side. If you fail, Knight of the Outer Void attacks you.

name: Call to Order
text: Revelation - Find the 2 topmost [[Cultist]] enemies in the encounter discard pile and spawn them in the empty location with the most remaining clues. If no [[Cultist]] enemies are spawned by this effect, Call to Order gains surge.

name: Expulsion
text: Revelation - If there are no [[Cultist]] enemies in play, Expulsion gains surge. Otherwise, the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy readies, moves (one location at a time) until it reaches your location, engages you, and makes an immediate attack. Then, place each ([skull]/[cultist]/[tablet]/[elder_thing]) key you control on that enemy.

name: Beneath the Lodge
text: If you control at least 1 ([skull]/[cultist]/[tablet]/[elder_thing]) key, Beneath the Lodge gains peril and its skill test gets +1 difficulty. Revelation - Test [intellect] (3). For each point you fail by, you must either lose 1 clue or take 1 horror.

name: Mark of the Order
text: Surge. Revelation - Check which investigator controls each key. The investigator with... - ...the [skull] key takes 1 damage. - ...the [cultist] key takes 1 horror. - ...the [tablet] key discards 2 random cards from hand. - ...the [elder_thing] key loses 3 resources.

name: August Lindquist
text: [action] Investigators at August Lindquist's location spend 2 [per_investigator] clues, as a group: Parley. Each investigator who spent at least 1 clue must either take 1 damage or 1 horror. Remove August Lindquist from the game and take control of his key.

name: Puzzle Box
text: Forced - When you are defeated: Give control of the Puzzle Box to another investigator. [action]: Unlight a brazier at your location, exhaust The Spectral Watcher, or deal 5 damage to The Spectral Watcher if it is exhausted. This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity from The Spectral Watcher. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Hallowed Mirror
text: Limit 1 per deck. Forced - After Hallowed Mirror enter play: Search your bonded cards for 3 copies of Soothing Melody. Add 1 to your hand and shuffle the other 2 into your deck. When Hallowed Mirror leaves play, find each of those copies of Soothing Melody ( even if they are out of play ) and set them aside, out of play.

name: Soothing Melody
text: Bonded (Hallowed Mirror). Heal 2 damage or 2 horror (or any combination thereof) from among investigators and/or [[Ally]] assets at your location. Draw 1 card.

name: "I've had worse…"
text: Fast. Play when you are dealt damage and/or horror. Cancel up to 2 damage and/or horror just dealt to you. Then, gain that many resources.

name: Occult Lexicon
text: Limit 1 per deck. Forced - After Occult Lexicon enters play: Search your bonded cards for 3 copies of Blood-Rite. Add 1 to your hand and shuffle the other 2 into your deck. When Occult Lexicon leaves play, find each of those copies of Blood-Rite (even if they are out of play) and set them aside, out of play.

name: Blood-Rite
text: Bonded (Occult Lexicon). Draw 2 cards. Discard up to 2 cards from your hand. For each card thusly discarded, you may either gain 1 resource, or spend 1 resource to deal 1 damage to an enemy at your location. This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

name: Glimpse the Unthinkable
text: Shuffle any number of non-weakness cards from your hand into your deck. Draw cards until you reach your maximum hand-size. Remove Glimpse the Unthinkable from the game.

name: "You owe me one!"
text: Look at another investigator's hand. You may play a non-weakness card in that investigator's hand, under your control. If you do, you and that investigator each draw 1 card.

name: Double, Double
text: Exceptional. [reaction] After you play an event, exhaust Double, Double: Play that event again as if it were in your hand.

name: Wither
text: [action]: Fight. This attack uses [willpower] instead of [combat]. You get +2 [willpower] for this attack. If a [skull], [cultist], [tablet], or [elder_thing] symbol is revealed during this attack, the attacked enemy gets -1 fight, -1 health, and -1 evade for the remainder of the turn (to a minimum of 1).

name: Sixth Sense
text: [action]: Investigate. Investigate using [willpower] instead of [intellect]. You get +2 [willpower] for this investigation. If a [skull], [cultist], [tablet], or [elder_thing] symbol is revealed during this test, you may choose a revealed location up to 2 connections away from your current location; you are now investigating as if you were at the chosen location in addition to your location (you may use either shroud value).

name: Lure
text: Attach to your location or to a connecting location. During the enemy phase, each enemy that moves does so along the shortest path toward the attached location, instead of to where it would normally move. Forced - While attached, at the end of the round: Discard Lure.

name: Eucatastrophe
text: Fast. Play when you reveal a chaos token that would reduce your skill value to 0 during a skill test (including the [auto_fail] token). Cancel that token and treat it as an [elder_sign] token, instead.

name: The Wages of Sin
text: Easy / Standard [skull]: -X. X is 1 higher than the number of copies of Unfinished Business in the victory display. [cultist]: -3. Until the end of the round, each Heretic enemy in play gets +1 fight and +1 evade. [tablet]: -3. If you fail, trigger the forced ability on a copy of Unfinished Business in yout threat area as if it were the end of the round. [elder_thing]: -2. If you fail and this is an attack or evasion attempt, resolve each haunted ability on your location.

name: Death's Approach
text: Locations cannot be flipped to their non-[[Spectral]] side.

name: In Pursuit of the Dead
text: Locations cannot be flipped to their [[Spectral]] side.

name: In Pursuit of the Living
text: [fast]: Flip your location over. (Group limit once per round at each location.) Clues cannot be discovered at non-[[Spectral]] locations. Objective - Banish as many Heretics as you can. If there are 4 copies of Unfinished Business in the victory display, advance.

name: Hangman's Brook
text: [action]: Resign. "Whose bright idea was this, anyway?" Haunted - Take 1 damage. Until the end of the round, the above resign ability cannot be triggered.

name: Hangman's Brook
text: [action]: Resign. "Whose bright idea was this, anyway?"

name: Haunted Fields
text: Each [[Spectral]] enemy at Haunted Fields gets +1 horror value. Haunted - Move the nearest [[Spectral]] enemy once toward Haunted Fields.

name: Haunted Fields
text: Each [[Spectral]] enemy at Haunted Fields gets +1 horror value.

name: Abandoned Chapel
text: During the mythos phase, each investigator in Abandoned Chapel gets -1 to each skill. Haunted - Until the end of the round, you get -1 to each skill.

name: Abandoned Chapel
text: During the mythos phase, each investigator in Abandoned Chapel gets -1 to each skill.

name: The Gallows
text: The Gallows gets +1 shroud for each [[Witch]] enemy in play. Haunted - Discard the top 3 cards of the standard encounter deck. If a [[Witch]] enemy is discarded by this effect, draw it.

name: The Gallows
text: The Gallows gets +1 shroud for each [[Witch]] enemy in play.

name: The Gallows
text: X is the number of [[Geist]] enemies in play. Haunted - Discard the top 3 cards of the spectral encounter deck. If a [[Geist]] enemy is discarded by this effect, draw it.

name: The Gallows
text: X is the number of [[Geist]] enemies in play.

name: Heretics' Graves
text: While investigating Heretics' Graves, add your [willpower] value to your skill value for the investigation. Haunted - Reduce your base [willpower] to 1 until the end of your next turn.

name: Heretics' Graves
text: While investigating Heretics' Graves, add your [willpower] value to your skill value for the investigation.

name: Heretics' Graves
text: Forced - After a [[Witch]] enemy at Heretics' Graves is defeated: Discard the top 2 cards of the standard encounter deck. Haunted - Heal 1 damage from each Heretic and each [[Witch]] enemy in play.

name: Heretics' Graves
text: Forced - After a [[Witch]] enemy at Heretics' Graves is defeated: Discard the top 2 cards of the standard encounter deck.

name: Chapel Crypt
text: While there are no ready enemies at Chapel Crypt, it gets -3 shroud. Haunted - Spawn the top card of your deck facedown, engaged with you. Treat that card as a Reanimated Dead enemy with 1 fight, 1 health, 1 evade, 1 damage, and the [[Monster]] trait.

name: Chapel Crypt
text: While there are no ready enemies at Chapel Crypt, it gets -3 shroud.

name: Chapel Crypt
text: While there are no [[Hex]] treacheries among investigators' threat areas at Chapel Crypt, it gets -3 shroud. Haunted - Find the topmost [[Hex]] treachery in the standard encounter discard pile and put it into play in your threat area.

name: Chapel Crypt
text: While there are no [[Hex]] treacheries among investigators' threat areas at Chapel Crypt, it gets -3 shroud.

name: Chapel Attic
text: Forced - After you draw a non-weakness card from your deck while at Chapel Attic: Place that card facedown beneath Chapel Attic, out of play. [reaction] After you successfully investigate Chapel Attic: Add each card beneath it to its owner's hand. Haunted - Discard a random card from beneath Chapel Attic.

name: Chapel Attic
text: Forced - After you draw a non-weakness card from your deck while at Chapel Attic: Place that card facedown beneath Chapel Attic, out of play. [reaction] After you successfully investigate Chapel Attic: Add each card beneath it to its owner's hand.

name: Chapel Attic
text: While investigating Chapel Attic, add the number of cards in your hand to your skill value for the investigation. Haunted - Discard a random card from your hand (2 cards instead of you have 5 or more cards in hand).

name: Chapel Attic
text: While investigating Chapel Attic, add the number of cards in your hand to your skill value for the investigation.

name: Spectral Web
text: [action] Investigators at your location spend 1-3 clues, as a group: Fight. Use this ability only to attack a [[Geist]] enemy. You may choose to use your [willpower] instead of your [combat] for this attack. You get +X skill value and deal +X damage for this attack, where X is the amount of clues spent to trigger this ability.

name: Heretic
text: Heretic gets +2 [per_investigator] health. While Heretic is at a non-[[Spectral]] location, it gains aloof and cannot be engaged or damaged. [fast] Spend 1 clue: Parley. Look at Heretic's other side (without resolving its text) . Forced - After Heretic is defeated: Flip it over and resolve the text on its other side.

name: Unfinished Business
text: The thing moans and wails with sorrow, in between hollow, echoing sobs. There must be some way you can bring it peace. Keep this card in your threat area (this side faceup). If you are eliminated, flip it over. Forced - At the end of the round: You must either take 1 horror or flip this card back to its enemy side. [action] If you are at Chapel Crypt and it has no clues on it: Test [intellect] or [combat] (4). If you succeed, the ghost is "banished" (resolve the text below). Do not read until this ghost is "banished." You find a nameless coffin and remove the lid. The spirit weeps as it reaches out to touch the body inside, remembering the love it once held in its heart. Add this card to the victory display.

name: Heretic
text: Heretic gets +2 [per_investigator] health. While Heretic is at a non-[[Spectral]] location, it gains aloof and cannot be engaged or damaged. [fast] Spend 1 clue: Parley. Look at Heretic's other side (without resolving its text) . Forced - After Heretic is defeated: Flip it over and resolve the text on its other side.

name: Unfinished Business
text: The charred ghost thrashes and howls in agony, as though in a constant state of torment. You cannot ease its suffering, but perhaps you can bring it justice. Keep this card in your threat area (this side faceup). If you are eliminated, flip it over. Forced - At the end of the round: You must either take 1 damage or flip this card back to its enemy side. [action] If you are at The Gallows and it has no clues on it: Test [willpower] or [agility] (4). If you succeed, the ghost is "banished" (resolve the text below). Do not read until this ghost is "banished." The fire spreads quickly, consuming the old wooden platform hungrily. The spirit's form twitches with a strange satisfaction. Add this card to the victory display.

name: Heretic
text: Heretic gets +2 [per_investigator] health. While Heretic is at a non-[[Spectral]] location, it gains aloof and cannot be engaged or damaged. [fast] Spend 1 clue: Parley. Look at Heretic's other side (without resolving its text) . Forced - After Heretic is defeated: Flip it over and resolve the text on its other side.

name: Unfinished Business
text: The spirit searches its surroundings frantically and grasps at you with desperate eyes. It must have lost something important - but what? Keep this card in your threat area (this side faceup). If you are eliminated, flip it over. Forced - At the end of the round: You must either lose 2 resources or flip this card back to its enemy side. [action] If you are at Chapel Attic and it has no clues on it: Test [intellect] or [agility] (4). If you succeed, the ghost is "banished" (resolve the text below). Do not read until this ghost is "banished." In an old cobwebbed trunk, you find all sorts of items belonging to the deceased. The sight of a crow skull amulet dipped in metal causes the ghost to gasp. "I'm sorry, sister, I'm sorry..." it weeps. Add this card to the victory display.

name: Heretic
text: Heretic gets +2 [per_investigator] health. While Heretic is at a non-[[Spectral]] location, it gains aloof and cannot be engaged or damaged. [fast] Spend 1 clue: Parley. Look at Heretic's other side (without resolving its text) . Forced - After Heretic is defeated: Flip it over and resolve the text on its other side.

name: Unfinished Business
text: The wraith is mangled and corrupted nearly beyond recognition. Something has torn through its insides and ripped its soul to shreds. Keep this card in your threat area (this side faceup). If you are eliminated, flip it over. Forced - At the end of the round: You must either choose and discard 2 cards from your hand or flip this card back to its enemy side. [action] If you are at Heretics' Graves and it has no clues on it: Test [willpower] or [combat] (4). If you succeed, the ghost is "banished" (resolve the text below). Do not read until this ghost is "banished." Digging up the bones was hard work, but as the spirit peers into its open grave, you can see its humanoid shape clearly for the first time. Add this card to the victory display.

name: Heretic
text: Heretic gets +2 [per_investigator] health. While Heretic is at a non-[[Spectral]] location, it gains aloof and cannot be engaged or damaged. [fast] Spend 1 clue: Parley. Look at Heretic's other side (without resolving its text) . Forced - After Heretic is defeated: Flip it over and resolve the text on its other side.

name: Unfinished Business
text: The specter's voice is soothing and melodic. Its words seem to make sense. You can at last be at peace - all you have to do is take its hand. Flip this card back to its enemy side. It makes an immediate attack. For the remainder of the scenario, this enemy cannot be defeated by any means, its parley ability can only be activated while at a [[Spectral]] location, and it costs +1 [per_investigator] clues to trigger. The next time this side is resolved, the ghost is "banished" instead (resolve the text below). Forced - If you looked at this side by resolving this Heretic's parley ability: Resolve the above text as though you had just defeated this Heretic. Do not read until this ghost is "banished." You spurn the creature's offer, remembering once more why you have come here. It lets out an agonizing shriek and returns from whence it came. Add this card to the victory display.

name: Heretic
text: Heretic gets +2 [per_investigator] health. While Heretic is at a non-[[Spectral]] location, it gains aloof and cannot be engaged or damaged. [fast] Spend 1 clue: Parley. Look at Heretic's other side (without resolving its text) . Forced - After Heretic is defeated: Flip it over and resolve the text on its other side.

name: Unfinished Business
text: The spiteful wraith does not relent. Your words cannot reach it, but perhaps you can lay it to rest by force. Flip this card back to its enemy side. It makes an immediate attack. The next time it is defeated, it is "banished" (resolve the text below). Forced - If you looked at this side by resolving this Heretic's parley ability: Flip this card back to its enemy side. It makes an immediate attack. (Do not resolve any other text on this card.) Do not read until this ghost is "banished." With a final cry, the hellish spirit recoils upon itself and dissipates into thin air. Add this card to the victory display.

name: Vengeful Witch
text: Spawn - The Gallows or Heretics' Graves. Alert. Hunter. Forced - When Vengeful Witch is defeated: Deal its damage and horror to each investigator at its location, as direct damage/horror.

name: Malevolent Spirit
text: Spawn - Chapel Attic or Chapel Crypt. While Malevolent Spirit is at a [[Spectral]] location, it gains hunter and gets +1 damage value and +1 horror value.
