If a [skull], [cultist], [tablet], [elder_thing], or [auto_fail] symbol is revealed during this test, each investigator at your location takes 1 damage and loses 1 action.

name: Alien Instruments
text: [action]: Investigate. Choose an exhausted [[Creature]] or [[Monster]] enemy at your location. The difficulty of this skill test is equal to the chosen enemy’s evade value. If you succeed, deal 1 damage to the chosen enemy and discover 1 additional clue at your location.

name: Murder at the Excelsior Hotel
text: Easy / Standard [skull]: -X. X is the number of [[Guest]] enemies in play. [cultist]: -1. If there is an [[Innocent]] enemy in the victory display, reveal another chaos token. [tablet]: -3. You may place one of your clues on your location to treat this as a -1 instead. [elder_thing]: -3. If you fail and there is an [[Innocent]] enemy in the victory display, take 1 horror.

name: The Murder
text: If there is only 1 investigator in the game, this agenda gets +1 doom threshold.

name: Special Investigation
text: Forced - After an investigator at the same location as a ready [[Police]] enemy discovers 1 or more clues at that location or deals damage to a [[Humanoid]] enemy: Each ready [[Police]] enemy at that location engages that investigator and makes an immediate attack.

name: What Happened...?
text: [action] Spend 1 [per_investigator] clues as a group: Draw the top card of the Leads deck. Objective - If the investigators control two [[Lead]] assets, advance.

name: Following Leads
text: Objective - Learn more about your leads by placing clues on them (using abilities on some locations) . The more clues, the better. At the end of the round, you may choose to advance if each [[Lead]] asset has at least 1 [per_investigator] clues on it. (Hint: If you wish to present this evidence to the police, you may wish to have at least 2 [per_investigator] clues on each [[Lead]] asset, instead.)

name: Bloodstained Dagger
text: [action]: Fight. You get +2 [combat] for this attack. [action] Exhaust Bloodstained Dagger and take 1 horror: Fight. You get +2 [combat] and deal +1 damage for this attack. If this attack defeats an enemy, draw 1 card.

name: What Have You Done?
text: Revelation - Put What Have You Done? into play in your threat area. As an additional cost for you to parley, you must discard a card at random from your hand. [action]: Shuffle What Have You Done? into your deck.

name: Sergeant Monroe
text: Sergeant Monroe may be assigned damage and/or horror dealt to any investigator at his location, even if he is uncontrolled. [reaction] When Sergeant Monroe is dealt any amount of damage or horror, exhaust him: Deal that much damage to a non-[[Innocent]] enemy at his location. Any investigator at Sergeant Monroe's location may trigger this ability.

name: Arkham Officer
text: Aloof. Patrol (nearest location with clues). Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Move 1 clue to Arkham Officer from its location, flipping it to its doom side. [action]: Parley. Test [willpower] (3). If you succeed, either automatically evade Arkham Officer, or flip one of its doom to its clue side and take control of it.

name: Room 225
text: [action]: Test [willpower] (3). If you succeed, remember that the investigators "cleaned up the blood." [action]: Test [combat] (3). If you succeed, remember that the investigators "hid the body." [action]: Test [intellect] (3). If you succeed, remember that the investigators "tidied up the room."

name: Suite Balcony
text: [action]: Choose a [[Humanoid]] enemy at Suite Balcony and test [combat] or [agility] (4). If you succeed, take 1 direct horror and defeat the chosen enemy (if that enemy is [[Elite]], deal 2 damage to it instead). This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

name: Second Floor Hall
text: [reaction] After you enter this location: You may immediately move to a connecting location. If you do, reveal a random token from the chaos bag. If a [skull], [cultist], [tablet], [elder_thing], or [auto_fail] symbol is revealed, draw the top card of the encounter deck.

name: Foyer
text: [action]: Resign. You flee the scene of the crime. Forced - When you attempt to move out of the Foyer while there is at least 1 [[Guest]] enemy here: Test [agility] (X), where X is the number of [[Guest]] enemies here. If you fail, you must spend 1 additional action to move out of the Foyer.

name: Restaurant
text: [action] Spend 2 resources: You place an order while enduring the withering glares of the entire waitstaff. At the end of your turn, if you are still at the Restaurant, you may take 1 horror to heal 3 damage. (Limit once per game.) [fast] If each location in play is revealed and there are no clues on locations in play: Place 1 [per_investigator] clues on Restaurant (from the token bank.)

name: Hotel Roof
text: [action]: Test [agility] (4) or [combat] (4). If you succeed, move to either Room 212, Room 225, or Room 245. [action]: Test [willpower] (3). If you succeed, move any number of clues controlled by investigators at this location to Alien Device (if it is in play).

name: Room 212
text: [action]: Test [intellect] (3). If you succeed, move any number of clues controlled by investigators at this location to Sinister Solution (if it is in play).

name: Room 245
text: [action]: Test [intellect] (3). Only the top card of your discard pile may be committed to this skill test (place it on the bottom of your deck after this test resolves). If you succeed, move any number of clues controlled by investigators in Room 245 to Time-Worn Locket (if it is in play).

name: Office
text: [action]: Test [intellect] (0). For each point you succeed by, you may move 1 clue controlled by an investigator in the Office to Manager's key (if it is in play).

name: Basement
text: [action]: Test [intellect] (1). If you succeed, move a [[Humanoid]] enemy at any location once toward the Basement. [reaction] After you defeat an enemy in the Basement: Move any number of clues controlled by investigators at the Basement to Tome of Rituals (if it is in play).

name: Mr. Trombly
text: Spawn - Foyer. Hunter. Forced - After Mr. Trombly enters play: Immediately resolve the hunter and patrol keywords on each other [[Staff]] enemy in play.

name: Conspicuous Staff
text: Spawn - Nearest [[Crime Scene]] location. Prey - Most clues. Hunter.

name: Hotel Guest
text: Spawn - Nearest [[Hall]] location. Aloof. Patrol (nearest [[Crime Scene]] location). Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if Hotel Guest is at a [[Crime Scene]] location: Add 1 doom to Hotel Guest. [action]: Parley. Test [willpower] or [intellect] (3). If you succeed, discard Hotel Guest.

name: Noxious Fumes
text: Revelation - In player order, each investigator at your location must choose: - Test [agility] (3) to attempt to flee before the gas fills the room. If you succeed, move to a connecting location. If you fail, take 2 damage. - Test [combat] (3) to attempt to hold your breath. For each point you fail by, take 1 damage.

name: Driven to Madness
text: Revelation - If there are no [[Humanoid]] enemies in play, Driven to Madness gains surge. Otherwise, attach Driven to Madness to the nearest [[Humanoid]] enemy. Attached enemy gets +1 fight, +1 health, and +1 evade. Attached enemy loses aloof. Investigators cannot parley with attached enemy. Forced - After attached enemy is evaded: Discard Driven to Madness.

name: Blood on your Hands
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (2). Increase the difficulty of this test by 1 for each [[Innocent]] enemy in the victory display. If you fail, take 2 horror (if you are at a [[Crime Scene]] location, you must also choose and discard a card from your hand).

name: Incriminating Evidence
text: Revelation - Attach to the nearest non-[[Crime Scene]] location. Attached location gains the [[Crime Scene]] trait and gets +2 shroud. [reaction] When you successfully investigate attached location: Instead of discovering clues, discard Incriminating Evidence.

name: Violent Outburst
text: Revelation - If there are no [[Humanoid]] enemies in play, search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Humanoid]] enemy, draw it, and shuffle the encounter deck. Otherwise, the nearest [[Humanoid]] enemy readies, moves (one location at a time) until it reaches your location, engages you, and makes an immediate attack.

name: Alien Device
text: Revelation - Put into play in your play area. Forced - When you are defeated: Give control of Alien Device to another investigator.

name: Otherworldly Meddler
text: Retaliate. Forced - When Otherworldly Meddler would take damage from an attack: Remove 1 doom from Otherworldly Meddler. Then, reduce the damage dealt by 1. Forced - After an investigator is defeated: Add 3 doom to Otherworldly Meddler.

name: Encephalon Signal
text: Peril. Revelation - Place 1 doom on Otherworldly Meddler (if it is in play) and test [willpower] (4). If you fail, you must either (choose one): - Take 2 horror. - Deal 2 damage to an investigator or [[Humanoid]] enemy at your location.

name: Manager's Key
text: Revelation - Put into play in your play area. Forced - When you are defeated: Give control of Manager's Key to another investigator.

name: Hotel Manager
text: Massive. Retaliate. Each [[Guest]] enemy loses patrol and gains surge. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Add 1 [[Guest]] enemy at Hotel Manager's location to the victory display, then place 2 doom on Hotel Manager. Move each other [[Guest]] enemy one location toward Hotel Manager.

name: Hotel Security
text: Spawn - Nearest empty location. Hunter. Retaliate. If there is at least 1 [[Guest]] enemy in play, Hotel Security loses hunter and gains "Patrol (nearest location with a [[Guest]] enemy)."

name: Tome of Rituals
text: Revelation - Put into play in your play area. Forced - When you are defeated: Give control of Tome of Rituals to another investigator.

name: Dimensional Shambler
text: Hunter. Forced - After Dimensional Shambler deals you damage from its attack: Reveal a random token from the chaos bag. If an [auto_fail] symbol is revealed, it snatches you from this dimension; remove Dimensional Shambler from the game and you are defeated.

name: Cultist of the Enclave
text: Spawn - Basement. Hunter. Forced - After Cultist of the Enclave attacks: Reveal a random token from the chaos bag. If a [skull], [cultist], [tablet], [elder_thing], or [auto_fail] symbol is revealed, place 1 doom on the current agenda.

name: Sinister Solution
text: Revelation - Put into play in your play area. Forced - When you are defeated: Give control of Sinister Solution to another investigator.

name: Harvested Brain
text: Forced - At the end of the investigation phase: Each investigator at attached location takes 1 damage and 1 horror. Each other investigator must either take 1 damage or 1 horror (their choice) .

name: Morbid Awareness
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (6). Reduce the difficulty of this test by 1 for each location away from Room 212 you are. If you fail, you must either place 1 of your clues on your location, or take 2 horror.

name: Time-Worn Locket
text: Revelation - Put into play in your play area. Forced - When you are defeated: Give control of Time-Worn Locket to another investigator.

name: Vengeful Specter
text: Patrol (Room 245). Retaliate. Forced - When damage would be placed on Vengeful Specter: Reduce that damage to 1 unless it was dealt by a [[Spell]], [[Relic]], [[Charm]], or encounter card.

name: Chilling Presence
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (3). For each point you fail by, take 1 horror. For each point you succeed by, you may deal 1 damage to a [[Geist]] enemy at your location. If an [elder_sign] symbol is revealed during this test, you may deal 2 damage to a [[Geist]] enemy at any location.

name: The True Culprit (v. I)
text: Alien Device gains: "[action] If Vengeful Specter is exhausted and at your location, remove 2 clues from Alien Device: Deal 2 damage to Vengeful Specter." Objective - If Vengeful Specter is defeated, advance.

name: The True Culprit (v. II)
text: Sinister Solution gains: "[action] If you are in Room 212: Test [intellect] (2). If you succeed, you may remove 2 clues from Sinister Solution to either exhaust Otherworldly Meddler or deal it 2 damage." Objective - If Otherworldly Meddler is defeated, advance.

name: The True Culprit (v. III)
text: Alien Device gains: "[action] Exhaust Alien Device and remove 2 clues from it: Add a [[Staff]] enemy at your location to the victory display (or deal it 2 damage if it is [[Elite]]). Does not provoke attacks of opportunity." Objective - If Hotel Manager is defeated, advance.

name: The True Culprit (v. IV)
text: Tome of Rituals gains: "[action] Remove 2 clues from Tome of Rituals: Fight. This attack uses [willpower] instead of [combat]. If you succeed, instead of dealing damage, remove 3 doom from the attacked enemy." Objective - If Otherworldly Meddler is defeated or has no doom on it, advance.

name: The True Culprit (v. V)
text: Sinister Solution gains: "[action] If Vengeful Specter is at your location: Test [intellect] (1). For each point you succeed by, move 1 clue from Sinister Solution to Vengeful Specter. Does not provoke attacks of opportunity." Objective - If Vengeful Specter is defeated or has clues on it equal to its remaining health, advance.

name: The True Culprit (v. VI)
text: Time-Worn Locket gains: "[reaction] When Hotel Manager would attack an investigator at your location, remove 2 clues from Time-Worn Locket: Cancel that attack." Objective - If Hotel Manager is defeated, advance.

name: The True Culprit (v. VII)
text: Each [[Guest]] enemy loses Victory 0 , loses [[Innocent]], and gains [[Cultist]]. Each [[Lead]] asset gains: "[action] If Dimensional Shambler is at your location, remove 1 clue from this asset: Evade. Automatically evade Dimensional Shambler. Objective - If there are no [[Cultist]] enemies in play, advance.

name: The True Culprit (v. VIII)
text: Each [[Lead]] asset gains: "[action] Remove 1 clue from this asset: Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Staff]] enemy and draw it. Shuffle the encounter deck." [reaction] After a [[Staff]] enemy is defeated: Deal 1 horror to Harvested Brain. Objective - If there is 2 plus 1 [per_investigator] horror on Harvested Brain, advance.

name: The True Culprit (v. IX)
text: Room 212 gains: "[action] If Tome of Rituals is at this location: Test [willpower] (5) or [intellect] (5). If you succeed, move up to 1 [per_investigator] clues from Tome of Rituals to Harvested Brain. Objective - If Harvested Brain has 3 [per_investigator] clues on it, advance

name: The True Culprit (v. X)
text: Each [[Guest]] enemy loses Victory 0 , loses [[Innocent]], and gains [[Cultist]]. Basement gains: "[action]: Test [willpower] (4) or [agility] (4). You may remove 1 clue from a [[Lead]] asset in the Basement to reduce the difficulty of this test by 2. If you succeed, remove 1 doom from the agenda." Objective - If there is no doom on this agenda, advance.

name: Return to The Gathering
text: Perform the setup as indicated in the Night of the Zealot Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The Gathering shown here. - Remove Act 1a- "Trapped" from the game and replace it with the new Act 1a- "Mysterious Gateway." (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Mysterious Gateway
text: Objective - Only investigators in the Guest Hall may spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.

name: Study
text: [action] [action]: Draw 3 cards. Only the lead investigator can activate this ability. Forced - When an enemy attempts to spawn at a location that is not in play: Put that location into play and spawn that enemy there.

name: Guest Hall
text: Investigators in this location cannot take draw actions.

name: Bedroom
text: Forced - After you fail a skill test while investigating the Bedroom: Discard 1 card at random from your hand.

name: Bathroom
text: Forced - After you reveal a [skull], [cultist], [tablet], or [auto_fail] symbol while investigating the Bathroom: Lose all remaining actions and end your turn.

name: Hallway
text: Forced - After you reveal this location: Put the set-aside Attic, Cellar, and Parlor locations into play.

name: Attic
text: Forced - After you reveal the Attic: Put the set-aside Far Above Your House location into play.

name: Field of Graves
text: Forced - After you reveal Field of Graves: Test [willpower] (4). For each point you fail by, discard 1 card at random from each player's hand.

name: Cellar
text: Forced - After you reveal the Cellar: Put the set-aside Deep Below Your House location into play.

name: Ghoul Pits
text: Forced - After you reveal Ghoul Pits: Test [agility] (3). For each point you fail by, search the encounter deck and discard pile for a copy of Swarm of Rats and draw it. Shuffle the encounter deck.

name: Corpse-Hungry Ghoul
text: Spawn - Bedroom. Hunter.

name: Ghoul from the Depths
text: Spawn - Bathroom. Retaliate.

name: The Zealot's Seal
text: Revelation - Each investigator with 3 or fewer cards in hand must take 1 damage and 1 horror. Each investigator with 4 or more cards in hand tests [willpower] (2). Each investigator who fails must discard 2 cards at random from his or her hand.

name: Return to The Midnight Masks
text: Perform the setup as indicated in the Night of the Zealot Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to the Midnight Masks shown here. - Construct the act deck using one of the two copies of Agenda 1a- "Predator or Prey" at random, without looking at their other sides. Remove the other version of Agenda 1a from the game. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Predator or Prey?
text: [action]: Resign. You don't want to risk taking too long, so you head to safety with the information you've gathered.

name: Narôgath
text: Spawn - Engaged with Prey. Prey - Nearest to another [[Cultist]] enemy. Hunter. Narôgath gets +3 health per investigator. While Narôgath is ready, each investigator at its location or a connecting location cannot Parley with [[Cultist]] enemies.

name: Easttown
text: [action]: Add 2 supply tokens or ammo tokens to an asset you control. (Limit once per game.)

name: Northside
text: [action]: Move to any [[Arkham]] location. (Limit once per game.)

name: Miskatonic University
text: [action] Take 2 horror: Gain 1 clue from the token pool. (Limit once per game.)

name: Rivertown
text: [action] Discard a card with X [willpower] icons: Remove X doom from a [[Cultist]] enemy in play. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Masked Horrors
text: Revelation - Each investigator with 2 or more clues takes 2 horror. If no horror is dealt by this effect, place 1 doom on the current agenda. This effect can cause the current agenda to advance.

name: Return to The Devourer Below
text: Perform the setup as indicated in the Night of the Zealot Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The Devourer Below shown here. - When choosing 4 Arkham Woods locations to put into play at random, include the 4 new Arkham Woods locations included in this encounter set, for a total of 10 possibilities. - After setting the Umôrdhoth enemy aside, flip this card over and attach it to Umôrdhoth.

name: Vault of Earthly Demise
text: Vault of Earthly Demise cannot leave play. Umôrdhoth gets +X health per investigator and gets +X fight, where X is the number of resources on this card. Forced - When Umôrdhoth enters play, place 1 resource on this card for each act remaining in the act deck.

name: Arkham Woods
text: Forced - After an investigator at this location succeeds at a skill test on a treachery: That treachery gains surge. (Group limit once per round.)

name: Arkham Woods
text: Forced - After you reveal a chaos token from the chaos bag while investigating this location: Reveal and resolve an additional chaos token. (Limit once per test.)

name: Arkham Woods
text: Each enemy at this location cannot take more than 1 damage from each attack performed against it.

name: Arkham Woods
text: Forced - After you reveal a chaos token from the chaos bag during an evasion attempt at this location: Reveal and resolve an additional chaos token. (Limit once per test.)

name: Umôrdhoth's Hunger
text: Revelation - Each investigator must discard 1 card at random from his or her hand. Each investigator who has no cards in his or her hand is killed. Heal 1 damage from each enemy in play.

name: Grave-Eater
text: Forced - After Grave-Eater attacks you: Discard 1 card at random from your hand.

name: Acolyte of Umôrdhoth
text: Prey - Fewest cards in hand. While engaged with an investigator with no cards in his or her hand, Acolyte of Umôrdhoth cannot be evaded.

name: Chill from Below
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (3). For each point you fail by, you must discard 1 card at random from your hand. For each card you cannot discard, take 1 damage.

name: Disciple of the Devourer
text: Spawn - Farthest empty location. Forced - After you spawn Disciple of the Devourer: You must either place 1 doom on it or place 1 of your clues on its location. If the current agenda is not agenda 1, do both instead.

name: Corpse-Taker
text: Spawn - Farthest empty location. Forced - At the end of the mythos phase: Place 1 doom on Corpse-Taker. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Corpse-Taker moves once toward Rivertown or Main Path. If Corpse-Taker is already at Rivertown or Main Path, move all doom from Corpse-Taker to the current agenda, instead.

name: Mask of Umôrdhoth
text: Revelation - Attach to the farthest [[Cultist]] enemy and place 1 doom on that enemy. If there are no [[Cultist]] enemies in play, search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Cultist]] enemy, draw it, and attach Mask of Umôrdhoth to it. Shuffle the encounter deck. Attached enemy gets +2 health. If attached enemy is unique, it gains retaliate. If it is non-unique, it gains aloof instead.

name: Jeremiah Pierce
text: Spawn - Your House. Otherwise, Rivertown. [action]: Parley. Add Jeremiah Pierce to the victory display. Then, test [willpower] (4). Place 1 doom on the current agenda for each point you fail by (this can cause the current agenda to advance).

name: Billy Cooper
text: Spawn - Easttown. Forced - After a [[Monster]] enemy is defeated at Billy Cooper's location: Add Billy Cooper to the victory display.

name: Alma Hill
text: Spawn - Southside. [action]: Parley. Draw the top 3 cards of the encounter deck. Then, add Alma Hill to the victory display.

name: Return to The Untamed Wilds
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Forgotten Age Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The Untamed Wilds shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Return to The Doom of Eztli
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Forgotten Age Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The Doom of Eztli shown here. - Gather the 3 Pit Viper cards from the Serpents encounter set and set them aside, out of play. - Before setup, remove the original Burial Pit, Underground Ruins, and Secret Passage locations from the game. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Harbinger of Valusia
text: Alert. Hunter. Retaliate. While this enemy has 5 [per_investigator] or more damage on it, it gets +2 fight and -2 evade. Forced - After a successful attack or evasion attempt against this enemy ends: Place 1 resource on it. Then, if there are 2 [per_investigator] resources on it, it vanishes with a sinister hiss. (Set it aside, out of play. It keeps all damage tokens.)

name: Entryway
text: [action]: Resign. You've escaped... for now. [action]: Check your supplies. If you have torches: Look at the top 2 cards of the exploration deck. Discard 1 treachery or enemy looked at by this effect. Shuffle the exploration deck. This ability can be used at any location.

name: Sealed Passage
text: [action] Check your supplies. If you have the Key of Eztli: Search the exploration deck and all set-aside cards for Chamber of Time and put it into play. If it is act 1a, skip directly to act 2a. For the remainder of this act, Sealed Passage is connected to Chamber of Time and vice versa. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Mosaic Chamber
text: [action] Check your supplies. If you have a canteen: The chamber accepts your gift of water. Remove 1 doom from any location in play. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Tomb of the Ancients
text: [action] Check your supplies. If an investigator at Tomb of the Ancients has rope: Move to a revealed location up to 3 connections away. Until the end of the round, Tomb of the Ancients is considered to be connected to that location, and vice versa. (Group limit once per round.)

name: Throne Room
text: While Throne Room has doom on it, it is considered to be in the victory display (for the purposes of counting vengeance). [action] If there are no clues or doom on Throne Room: Record "mysterious scepter" in your supplies. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Snake Pit
text: Forced - After you put Snake Pit into play by exploring successfully, but before moving to it: Spawn 1 [per_investigator] set-aside Pit Viper enemies in Snake Pit (to a maximum of 3). Check your supplies. If an investigator at your location has binoculars, you may choose not to move to Snake Pit. Otherwise, you must move to it.

name: Ancient Hall
text: Ancient Hall enters play with 1 [per_investigator] doom on it. [action]: Flip 1 doom on Ancient Hall to its clue side. [fast]: Check your supplies. If you have a compass, flip 1 doom on Ancient Hall to its clue side. (Group limit once per round.)

name: Grand Chamber
text: [action] Check your supplies. If you have a map and there are no clues on Grand Chamber: For the remainder of the scenario (and its resolution) , Grand Chamber loses Vengeance 1 and gains Victory 1 . (Group limit once per game.)

name: Chamber of Time
text: Forced - After Chamber of Time enters play: Attach the set-aside Relic of Ages asset to the Chamber of Time. Place 1 doom on Chamber of Time.

name: Return to Threads of Fate
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Forgotten Age Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Threads of Fate shown here. - Set the Veda Whitsley story asset aside, out of play. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Search for the Meaning
text: Objective - Only investigators in Rivertown can spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.

name: The Hastings Estates
text: Revelation - Put The Hastings Estate into play. [action] Cross medicine off from your supplies: Discover all of the clues on The Hastings Estate.

name: Search for the Source
text: Objective - Only investigators in Northside can spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.

name: Merle Garvin
text: Revelation - Put Merle Garvin into play at Miskatonic University. [action]: Parley. Mr. Garvin tries to give you the slip. Test [agility] (2). If you succeed, place 1 clue on Merle Garvin (from the token pool) and move him 1 location away from you. If you fail, move him 2 locations away from you.

name: The Overgrown Estate
text: Objective - Only investigators at The Hastings Estate can spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.

name: The Overgrown Estate
text: Objective - Only investigators at The Hastings Estate can spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.

name: Clinton Freeman
text: Revelation - Put Clinton Freeman into play at Velma's Diner. Advance to Act 3g - "Discover the Truth." [action]: Parley. Test [willpower] or [intellect] (3). If you succeed, place 1 clue on Clinton Freeman (from the token pool) and take 1 horror. If you fail, take 1 horror for each point you failed by.

name: Seeking Trouble
text: Objective - If there are 1 [per_investigator] clues on Merle Garvin: You must remove them and immediately advance.

name: Seeking Trouble
text: Objective - If there are 1 [per_investigator] clues on Merle Garvin: You must remove them and immediately advance.

name: Loading Docks
text: Revelation - Put Loading Docks into play with 4 resources on it. Advance to Act 3g - "Discover the Truth." While there are no resources on Loading Docks, clues on it cannot be discovered by any means. Forced - At the end of the round: Remove 1 resource from Loading Docks.

name: Discover the Truth
text: Objective - If there are 1 [per_investigator] clues on Clinton Freeman: You must remove them and immediately advance. Objective - If Loading Docks is in play and there are no clues remaining on it: You must immediately advance.

name: Impossible Pursuit
text: Objective - If the Harbinger of Valusia leaves play, advance.

name: Veda Whitsley
text: [fast] Exhaust Veda Whitsley: Look at the top card of the exploration deck or encounter deck. If it is an enemy, you may deal 1 damage to Veda Whitsley to discard it. If it is a treachery, you may deal 1 horror to Veda Whitsley to discard it. Otherwise, return it to the top of its deck.

name: Return to The Boundary Beyond
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Forgotten Age Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The Boundary Beyond shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Temple Ruins
text: [reaction] After you successfully investigate Temple Ruins: Explore. Draw the top card of the exploration deck. If it is a location with a matching location symbol in the upper-left corner, place it on top of this location, taking its place.

name: Metropolitan Cathedral
text: [action] Take 1 horror: Gain 2 resources. [action] If you have 10 or more resources: Explore. Draw the top card of the exploration deck. If it is a location with a matching location symbol in the upper-left corner, place it on top of this location, taking its place.

name: Chapultepec Park
text: Forced - After you fail an [agility] test while at Chapultepec Park: Take 1 damage. [action]: Explore. Test [agility] (3). If you succeed, draw the top card of the exploration deck. If it is a location with a matching location symbol in the upper-left corner, place it on top of this location, taking its place.

name: Zócalo
text: [action] Discard cards with a combined total of at least 4 skill icons from your hand: Explore. Draw the top card of the exploration deck. If it is a location with a matching location symbol in the upper-left corner, place it on top of this location, taking its place.

name: Xochimilco
text: [reaction] After you successfully fight or evade an enemy at Xochimilco: Explore. Draw the top card of the exploration deck. If it is a location with a matching location symbol in the upper-left corner, place it on top of this location, taking its place.

name: Coyoacán
text: While you are at Coyoacán, damage/horror cannot be healed from cards you control. [action] If you have 5 or more total damage and/or horror on your investigator: Explore. Draw the top card of the exploration deck. If it is a location with a matching location symbol in the upper-left corner, place it on top of this location, taking its place.

name: Return to Heart of the Elders, Part 1
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Forgotten Age Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Heart of the Elders, Part 1 shown here. - Remove Act 1a - "Search for the Pattern" from the game and replace it with the new Act 1a - "A Familiar Pattern." (Continued on reverse side.)

name: A Familiar Pattern
text: Objective - When the investigators have collected the requisite number of clues, they must immediately spend them and advance.

name: The Winged Serpent
text: Spawn - Mouth of K'n-yan. Hunter. Massive. Cannot be defeated. Cannot make attacks of opportunity. Forced - If there are 5 or more damage on this enemy, or after a pillar token is placed on the Mouth of K'n-yan: Heal all damage from this enemy and exhaust it. It does not ready during the upkeep phase this round.

name: Feathered Serpent
text: X is the number of pillar tokens on Mouth of K'n-yan. While X is 3 or higher, Feathered Serpent gains alert and hunter. [action]: Evade. Check your supplies. If you have a mysterious scepter, you automatically evade the serpent and command it to deal its damage to another enemy at its location.

name: Return to Heart of the Elders, Part 2
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Forgotten Age Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Heart of the Elders, Part 2 shown here. - After setup, search the collection for the new Harbinger of Valusia (The Sleeper Returns) and set it aside, out of play. If the Harbinger of Valusia would enter play during this scenario, use this version instead of the original one. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Ruins of K'n-yan
text: [action] [action]: Check your supplies. If you have a compass, discover 2 clues on Ruins of K'n-yan. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Subterranean Swamp
text: [fast] If there are no clues on Subterranean Swamp: Check your supplies. If you have a canteen, place 1 doom on this location and record "sticky goop" in your supplies. (Group limit once per game.) Forced - After you fail a skill test while investigating Subterranean Swamp: Check your supplies. If you do not have a pocketknife, lose all remaining actions and end your turn.

name: Chthonian Depths
text: Forced - After the last clue is discovered from Chthonian Depths: Check your supplies. If no investigator at this location has torches, place 1 doom on it.

name: Treacherous Descent
text: [action]: Check your supplies. If you have a pickaxe, reduce the shroud value of Treacherous Descent by 2 for the remainder of the scenario. (Group limit 3 times per game.)

name: Return to The City of Archives
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Forgotten Age Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The City of Archives shown here. - Put Halls of Pnakotus (Southern Corridors) into play. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Unrestricted Access
text: Objective - If three of the following are true, you must advance: - An investigator "found the process." - An investigator "dissected an organ." - An investigator "interviewed a subject." - An investigator "realized what year it is." - An investigator "activated the device." - An investigator controls The Custodian. - An investigator "read about Earth." - An investigator "saw a familiar specimen."

name: Halls of Pnakotus
text: This location is connected to Halls of Pnakotus (Eastern Corridors) and Halls of Pnakotus (Western Corridors) , and vice versa. [fast] During your turn, choose and discard a card from your hand: Move to another copy of Halls of Pnakotus. (Limit once per turn.)

name: Cyclopean Vaults
text: [action]: Discard each non-weakness card in your hand. [action] If you have no cards in your hand: Remember that you "read about Earth." [action] [action] If you have fewer than 5 cards in your hand: Draw cards until you have exactly 5 cards in your hand.

name: Alien Conservatory
text: While you are in the Alien Conservatory, decrease your maximum hand size by 2. [action]: Test [willpower] (1). If you succeed, test [agility] (2). If you succeed, test [intellect] (3). If you succeed, remember that you "saw a familiar specimen."

name: Captive Subjects
text: Spawn - Alien Conservatory. Aloof. Retaliate. If an investigator "read about Earth" or "saw a familiar specimen," Captive Subjects loses aloof and gains hunter.

name: Return to The Depths of Yoth
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Forgotten Age Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The Depths of Yoth shown here. - After setup, search the collection for the new Harbinger of Valusia (The Sleeper Returns) and set it aside, out of play. If the Harbinger of Valusia would enter play during this scenario, use this version instead of the original one. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Perils of Yoth
text: Peril. Revelation - You must either (choose one): - Set Perils of Yoth aside, out of play. (This exploration fails.) - Set Perils of Yoth aside, out of play: take horror or damage equal to the current depth level; and continue exploring as if you had not drawn Perils of Yoth.

name: Return to Shattered Aeons
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Forgotten Age Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Shattered Aeons shown here. - When building the exploration deck, use only the 4 single-sided [[Otherworld]] locations. Do not include any of the other encounter cards listed in the original setup instructions. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Great Hall of Celeano
text: [reaction] After you move to Great Hall of Celeano, place 1 doom on it: Look at the top card of the encounter deck, the exploration deck, or any player deck. You may discard that card.

name: Buenos Aires
text: Buenos Aires gets -2 shroud for each doom on it. Forced - At the end of the mythos phase: Place 1 doom on Buenos Aires.

name: Ultima Thule
text: Forced - After Ultima Thule enters play: Search the chaos bag for the [elder_sign] chaos token and seal it on Ultima Thule.

name: Unknowable Past
text: Peril. Revelation - You must either (choose one): - Reveal 4 chaos tokens. For each [skull], [cultist], [tablet], [elder_thing], or [auto_fail] symbol revealed, take 1 damage and 1 horror. - Choose and discard 1 card from your hand and shuffle Unknowable Past into the exploration deck. You cannot choose this option if Unknowable Past was drawn from the exploration deck.

name: Return to Turn Back Time
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Forgotten Age Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Turn Back Time shown here. - Gather the 3 Pit Viper cards from the Serpents encounter set and set them aside, out of play. - Before setup, remove the original Burial Pit, Underground Ruins, and Secret Passage locations from the game. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Riverside Temple
text: Forced - After any number of clues are discovered at Riverside Temple: Check your supplies. If no investigator at Riverside Temple has chalk, shuffle an equal number of cards from the top of the encounter deck into the exploration deck.

name: Waterfall
text: [reaction] When you investigate Waterfall: Check your supplies. If an investigator at Waterfall has rope, reduce Waterfall's shroud by 3 for this investigation. Forced - After you fail a skill test while investigating Waterfall: Discard 1 card at random from your hand.

name: Trail of the Dead
text: Forced - After you investigate or explore while at Trail of the Dead: Check your supplies. If no investigator at Trail of the Dead has binoculars, take 1 horror.

name: Cloud Forest
text: Forced - When an enemy at Cloud Forest would be dealt any amount of damage: Check your supplies. If no investigator at Cloud Forest has torches, cancel that damage.

name: Brotherhood Acolyte
text: Spawn - Location with the most [[Cultist]] enemies. Aloof. Forced - After Brotherhood Acolyte enters plaay: Place 1 doom on each other [[Cultist]] enemy at its location.

name: Stolen Mind
text: Spawn - Any empty location. Retaliate. Stolen Mind gets +1 fight for each doom on it. Forced - At the end of the mythos phase: Place 1 doom on Stolen Mind.

name: From Another Time
text: Revelation - Test [intellect] (4). Place X doom on [[Cultist]] enemies in play, divided as evenly as possible. X is the amount you failed by. If you fail and there are no [[Cultist]] enemies in play, instead search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Cultist]] enemy and draw it (shuffle the encounter deck).

name: Resentful Wilds
text: Revelation - Take 1 horror and attach Resentful Wilds to the nearest location without a copy of Resentful Wilds attached. Forced - After you successfully explore at attached location: Add Resentful Wilds to the victory display.

name: Best-Laid Plans
text: Peril. Revelation - You must either (choose one): - Lose 2 actions. - Shuffle the top card of the encounter deck into the exploration deck.

name: Merging Timelines
text: Revelation - Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. For each card in your hand that matches one of the cards (by title) , discard that card from your hand and lose 1 action. Shuffle each weakness discarded by this effect into your deck.

name: Tindalos Alpha
text: Alert. Retaliate. Forced - After Tindalos Alpha attacks you: Shuffle a non-story asset you control into your deck, moving all damage and horror from that asset to your investigator. Discard Tindalos Alpha. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Discard Tindalos Alpha.

name: Vengeful Serpent
text: Hunter. Forced - After you draw Vengeful Serpent: Draw each Vengeful Serpent in the victory display as well.

name: Serpent Guardian
text: Spawn - Nearest location with at least 1 clue. Aloof. While Serpent Guardian is unengaged, its location gets +2 shroud. While there are 3 or more vengeance points in the victory display, Serpent Guardian loses aloof and gains hunter.

name: Wrath of Yig
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (2). This test gets +1 difficulty for each vengeance point in the victory display. If you fail, put a set-aside Poisoned weakness into play in your threat area (if you cannot, take 2 damage).

name: The Star • XVII
text: Each asset you control with health gets +1 health. Each asset you control with sanity gets +1 sanity. [reaction] When the game begins, if The Star • XVII is in your opening hand: Put it into play.

name: Hallowed Mirror
text: Limit 1 per deck. [reaction] When you play a copy of Soothing Melody: Either change each "2" on it to a "3," or shuffle it into your deck instead of discarding it. Forced - After Hallowed Mirror enters play: Search your bonded cards for 3 copies of Soothing Melody. Add 1 to your hand and shuffle the other 2 into your deck.

name: The World • XXI
text: [fast] If you have 8 or more cards in your hand, exhaust The World • XXI: Draw 1 card. [reaction] When the game begins, if The World • XXI is in your opening hand: Put it into play.

name: Occult Lexicon
text: Limit 1 per deck. [reaction] When you play a copy of Blood-Rite: Either change each "2" on it to a "3," or shuffle it into your deck instead of discarding it. Forced - After Occult Lexicon enters play: Search your bonded cards for 3 copies of Blood-Rite. Add 1 to your hand and shuffle the other 2 into your deck.

name: Knight of Swords
text: [reaction] When you would succeed at a skill test: You get +1 skill value for this test. You may discard Knight of Swords to get +3 instead. [reaction] When the game begins, if Knight of Swords is in your opening hand: Put it into play.

name: Well Connected
text: Limit 1 per investigator. [fast] Exhaust Well Connected: You get +1 skill value for this skill test for every 4 resources you have. [fast] Spend 2 resources: Ready Well Connected. (Limit once per round.)

name: The Hierophant • V
text: You have 1 additional arcane slot. Your arcane slots can be used to hold cards that take up accessory slots, and vice versa. [reaction] When the game begins, if The Hierophant • V is in your opening hand: Put it into play.

name: Sign Magick
text: Fast. You have 1 additional arcane slot, which can only be used to hold a [[Spell]] or [[Ritual]] asset. [reaction] After you activate an [action] ability on a [[Spell]] or [[Ritual]] asset, exhaust Sign Magick: Activate an [action] ability on a different [[Spell]] or [[Ritual]] asset you control (without paying its [action] cost) .

name: Nine of Rods
text: [reaction] When you draw a non-weakness encounter card, exhaust Nine of Rods: Cancel all of that card's effects and shuffle it into the encounter deck. Then, draw another card from the top of the encounter deck. [reaction] When the game begins, if Nine of Rods is in your opening hand: Put it into play.

name: Trial by Fire
text: Fast. Play only during your turn. Until the end of your turn, set the base value of each of your skills to 5, or the base value of one of your skills to 7.

name: The Fool • 0
text: [reaction] When you play a card, exhaust The Fool • 0: Reduce that card's cost by 1. [reaction] When the game begins, if The Fool • 0 is in your opening hand: Put it into play.

name: Moon Pendant
text: You have 1 additional tarot slot. Each non-weakness [[Tarot]] asset in your hand or committed to a skill test gains [wild] [wild]. [reaction] When the game begins, if Moon Pendant is in your opening hand: Put it into play.

name: Observed
text: Permanent. Limit 1 per investigator. Before setting up each game, draw 3 random cards from the tarot deck and choose 1. Place the chosen card in front of you, in the upright position. Each other investigator ignores its effects. Shuffle the rest back into the tarot deck.

name: Damned
text: Permanent. Before setting up each game, draw a random card from the tarot deck and place the chosen card in front of you, in the reverse position. Each other investigator ignores its effects.

name: The Devil • XV
text: You cannot play assets other than The Devil • XV while it is in your hand. If The Devil • XV is drawn in your opening hand during setup (before or after taking a mulligan) , you cannot replace it. It must stay in your opening hand.

name: Physical Training
text: [fast] Spend 1 resource: You get +1 [willpower] for this skill test. [fast] Spend 1 resource: You get +1 [combat] for this skill test.

name: Dynamite Blast
text: Choose either your location or a connecting location. Deal 3 damage to each enemy and to each investigator at the chosen location. This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

name: Hyperawareness
text: [fast] Spend 1 resource: You get +1 [intellect] for this skill test. [fast] Spend 1 resource: You get +1 [agility] for this skill test.

name: Barricade
text: Attach to your location. Non-[[Elite]] enemies cannot move into or spawn at attached location. Each time a non-[[Elite]] enemy would spawn at attached location, spawn it at a connecting location instead, if able. Forced - When an investigator leaves attached location: Discard Barricade.

name: Hard Knocks
text: [fast] Spend 1 resource: You get +1 [combat] for this skill test. [fast] Spend 1 resource: You get +1 [agility] for this skill test.

name: Hot Streak
text: Gain 10 resources.

name: Arcane Studies
text: [fast] Spend 1 resource: You get +1 [willpower] for this skill test. [fast] Spend 1 resource: You get +1 [intellect] for this skill test.

name: Mind Wipe
text: Fast. Play after a phase begins. Choose a non-[[Elite]] enemy in your location. Until the end of the phase, reduce the chosen enemy's damage and horror values by 1, and treat its printed text box as if it were blank (except for [[Traits]]).

name: Dig Deep
text: [fast] Spend 1 resource: You get +1 [willpower] for this skill test. [fast] Spend 1 resource: You get +1 [agility] for this skill test.

name: Rabbit's Foot
text: [reaction] After you fail a skill test, exhaust Rabbit's Foot: Search the top X cards of your deck for a card, draw it, and shuffle the remaining cards into your deck. X is the amount you failed by.

name: Return to Extracurricular Activities
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Dunwich Legacy Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Extracurricular Activities shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Warren Observatory
text: Forced - After you discover 1 or more clues at Warren Observatory: Discard 3 cards from the top of your deck for each clue just discovered.

name: Enthralled Security Guard
text: Hunter. Retaliate. Forced - After Enthralled Security Guard attacks you: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck. Forced - After Enthralled Security Guard is evaded: It is defeated.

name: Return to The House Always Wins
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Dunwich Legacy Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The House Always Win shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Clover Club Lounge
text: Forced - After Clover Club Lounge is revealed: Put the set-aside Clover Club Stage into play. While it is Act 1, Clover Club Lounge gains: "[action]: Look at the top card of your deck. If it is an [[Ally]] asset, you may put it into play. Otherwise, draw it. (Limit once per round.)"

name: Clover Club Stage
text: Clover Club Bar is connected to Clover Club Stage. While it is Act 1, Clover Club Stage gains: "[action] Spend 1 resource: Place that resource on Clover Club Stage. [fast] If there are 1 [per_investigator] resources on Clover Club Stage: Gain 1 [per_investigator] clues from the token pool. (Group limit once per game.)"

name: Caught Cheating
text: Surge. Revelation - Each player who has "cheated" must lose 2 resources and take 1 damage.

name: Raise the Stakes
text: Revelation - You must (choose one): - Remember that you have "cheated." - Lose 5 resources. - Put Raise the Stakes into play in your threat area. It gains: "Each [[Criminal]] enemy at your location loses aloof. If it is Act 2 or 3, each [[Criminal]] enemy engaged with you gets +1 fight and +1 evade."

name: Return to The Miskatonic Museum
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Dunwich Legacy Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The Miskatonic Museum shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Exhibit Hall
text: Forced - After you fail a skill test while investigating this location: Take 1 damage.

name: Exhibit Hall
text: [action] [action]: Gain 2 clues (from the token bank). Then, if Hunting Horror is in play, place 1 resource on Shadow-spawned. Otherwise, search the encounter deck, discard pile, and the void for Hunting Horror and spawn it in the Museum Halls. Shuffle the encounter deck.

name: Dark Bidding
text: Revelation - Attach Dark Bidding to Hunting Horror, even if it is out of play. Dark Bidding remains attached to Hunting Horror if it enters the void. Forced - When Hunting Horror attacks an investigator: That investigator must take 1 additional damage and 1 additional horror. Heal 2 damage from Hunting Horror. Discard Dark Bidding. (Max once per attack.)

name: Night Beyond Void
text: Revelation - Place 1 resource on Shadow-spawned, even if it is out of play.

name: Return to The Essex County Express
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Dunwich Legacy Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The Essex County Express shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: The Conductor
text: Spawn - Leftmost location. Hunter. Massive. Forced - When The Conductor is defeated: Attach it to the current agenda. After you advance the attached agenda, draw The Conductor. Forced - When The Conductor's location leaves play: Instead of discarding The Conductor, move it one location to the right.

name: Engine Car
text: Engine Car is connected to the location to the left of it. Engine Car cannot be investigated. Forced - After you reveal Engine Car: Put the set-aside Engineer into play at the Engine Car.

name: Freight Car
text: Freight Car is connected to the locations to the left and right of it. Forced - At the end of the round: Each investigator on the Freight Car must test [agility] (1). Each investigator who fails must test [agility] (1) again. Each investigator who fails this second test is pulled into the vortex above and is defeated.

name: Baggage Car
text: Baggage Car is connected to the locations to the left and right of it. Baggage Car cannot be investigated. [action] Choose and discard a card from your hand: Discover 1 clue in the Baggage Car.

name: Engineer
text: [action] If Engineer is ready: Parley. Test [willpower] or [intellect] (4). If you succeed, take control of Engineer. If you already control Engineer, discover 1 clue from your location instead. Any investigator at Engineer's location may trigger this action. Forced - When Engineer would be discarded: Instead, heal all damage and horror from it, lose control of it, and exhaust it.

name: Return to Blood on the Altar
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Dunwich Legacy Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Blood on the Altar shown here. - Set each copy of Hired Gun aside, out of play. - Remove the original Village Commons location from the game and replace it with the new Village Commons (Silent Decay) . (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Village Commons
text: Each investigator at Village Commons cannot take draw or resource actions. [action]: Resign. "This is suicide! We're better off hiding out the night."

name: Bishop's Brook
text: Forced - After you reveal Bishop's Brook: Test [agility] (4). If you fail, take 2 damage. [fast] If there are no clues here: Draw the encounter card underneath Bishop's Brook. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Burned Ruins
text: Forced - After you successfully investigate Burned Ruins: Take 1 damage. [fast] If there are no clues here: Draw the encounter card underneath Burned Ruins. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Osborn's General Store
text: Forced - After you reveal Osborn's General Store: Gain 2 resources. [fast] If there are no clues here: Draw the encounter card underneath Osborn's General Store. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Congregational Church
text: Forced - After you reveal Congregational Church: Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a copy of Kidnapped! and draw it. Shuffle the encounter deck. [fast] If there are no clues here: Draw the encounter card underneath Congregational Church. (Group limit once per game.)

name: House in the Reeds
text: While you are in the House in the Reeds, you cannot play assets. [fast] If there are no clues here: Draw the encounter card underneath House in the Reeds. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Schoolhouse
text: Forced - After you discover a clue from this location: Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a copy of Servant of Many Mouths and draw it. Shuffle the encounter deck. [fast] If there are no clues here: Draw the encounter card underneath Schoolhouse. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Hired Gun
text: Each [[Criminal]] enemy (including Hired Gun) gains: "Hunter. Prey - Most resources." Each copy of Hunted Down gains peril and surge.

name: Return to Undimensioned and Unseen
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Dunwich Legacy Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Undimensioned and Unseen shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Brood of Yog-Sothoth
text: Massive. Brood of Yog-Sothoth gets +1 [per_investigator] health and cannot be damaged or attacked except using the ability on Esoteric Formula. Forced - After Brood of Yog-Sothoth moves for the first time each round, if it is unengaged: It immediately moves again toward a random location.

name: Brood of Yog-Sothoth
text: Massive. Brood of Yog-Sothoth gets +2 [per_investigator] health and cannot be damaged or attacked except using the ability on Esoteric Formula.

name: Brood of Yog-Sothoth
text: Massive. Retaliate. Brood of Yog-Sothoth gets +1 [per_investigator] health and cannot be damaged or attacked except using the ability on Esoteric Formula.

name: Brood of Yog-Sothoth
text: Massive. Retaliate. Brood of Yog-Sothoth gets +1 [per_investigator] health and cannot be damaged or attacked except using the ability on Esoteric Formula. Forced - After Brood of Yog-Sothoth enters your location, or vice versa: Test [willpower] (3). If you fail, take 1 horror.

name: Imperceptible Creature
text: Surge. Revelation - Attach to a Brood of Yog-Sothoth enemy in play without a copy of Imperceptible Creature attached. Remove 1 clue from that enemy. Attached enemy gains [[Elite]].

name: Return to Where Doom Awaits
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Dunwich Legacy Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Where Doom Awaits shown here. - Set Naomi O'Bannion aside, out of play. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Base of the Hill
text: Base of the Hill is connected to each copy of Diverging Path. Forced - After you discover 1 or more clues from Base of the Hill: Spend those clues and put that many random set-aside Diverging Path locations into play. [action]: Resign. "This is more than I signed up for!"

name: Ascending Path
text: Ascending Path is connected to each copy of Altered Path. Forced - After you discover 1 or more clues from Ascending Path: Spend those clues and put that many random set-aside Altered Path locations into play.

name: Abandoned Camp
text: Forced - After you reveal Abandoned Camp: Lose 2 resources for each action you have performed this round (including this one, if applicable) .

name: Fathomless Lake
text: Forced - After you reveal Fathomless Lake: If you have 2 or more actions remaining, place 1 doom on the current agenda.

name: Naomi O'Bannion
text: You get +1 [intellect] and +1 [combat]. [reaction] When an investigator at your location reveals a non-[auto_fail] chaos token during an [intellect] or [combat] test, exhaust Naomi O'Bannion: Cancel that chaos token and reveal a new chaos token. For the remainder of this test, if another copy of the canceled chaos token is revealed, cancel it and reveal a new chaos token.

name: Return to Lost in Time and Space
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Dunwich Legacy Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Lost in Time and Space shown here. - Set Seth Bishop (Thrall of Yog-Sothoth) aside, out of play. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Breaking Through (v. II)
text: Forced - After you are moved to a location by an encounter card effect: Take 1 horror.

name: Into the Beyond (v. II)
text: [action]: Discard the top 3 cards of the encounter deck. Choose a location discarded by this effect and resolve its 'revelation' ability. Objective - If an investigator enters The Edge of the Universe, advance.

name: Seth Bishop
text: Retaliate. Forced - When Seth Bishop attacks you during the enemy phase: Either move to Another Dimension after this attack, or Seth Bishop deals +2 damage for this attack. Forced - After Yog-Sothoth enters Seth Bishop's location (or vice versa): Seth is devoured whole. Remove Seth Bishop from the game. Heal 6 damage from Yog-Sothoth.

name: Realms Beyond
text: Yog-Sothoth is immune to player card effects, cannot take damage, and cannot leave Realms Beyond. As an additional cost for you to leave Realms Beyond, test [willpower] or [agility] (3). If you fail, cancel the effects of the move. Forced - When a forced effect would move an investigator or enemy to Another Dimension: Move them here instead. Cannot be canceled.

name: Indecipherable Stairs
text: Revelation - Put Indecipherable Stairs into play and discard the top 3 cards of your deck. Forced - After you leave Indecipherable Stairs: You must either take 2 horror, or discard Indecipherable Stairs.

name: Towering Luminosity
text: Revelation - Put Towering Luminosity into play and either place 1 doom on it or take 2 damage. Forced - After you fail a test while at this location: You must either flip 1 clue on Towering Luminosity to its doom side, or discard Towering Luminosity.

name: Unstable Vortex
text: Revelation - Put Unstable Vortex into play and discard 1 copy of Tear Through Space, if able. Forced - At the end of your turn, if you are at Unstable Vortex: You must either draw the top card of the encounter deck, or shuffle Unstable Vortex into the encounter deck.

name: Haunting Recollections
text: Revelation - For each card in your hand, if there is a copy of that card in your discard pile, take 1 horror (to a maximum of 3 horror). If you take no horror from this effect, discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

name: A Baleful Welcome
text: Peril. Revelation - Choose two of the following actions (investigate, fight, evade, move, or play). For the remainder of the round, each investigator cannot perform each chosen action. If this is not the first copy of A Baleful Welcome drawn this phase, choose three of those actions instead.

name: Infinite Doorway
text: Revelation - Attach to your location. As an additional cost to move into or out of attached location, discard the top card of your deck. If the discarded card is a weakness, draw it. Otherwise, for each copy of the discarded card in your hand or play area, you must either take 1 horror or discard that card.

name: Resurgent Evils
text: Peril. Revelation - You must either (choose one): - Draw the top 2 cards of the encounter deck. - Place 1 doom on the current agenda. This effect can cause the current agenda to advance.

name: Secret Door
text: Revelation - Attach to the location with the most investigators, and without a Secret Door attached. Investigators cannot leave this location. [action]: Test [willpower] (3) to search for the passage or [intellect] (3) to discern the way out. If you succeed, discard Secret Door.

name: Inexplicable Cold
text: Revelation - Test [agility] (4). For each point you fail by, you must either choose and discard 1 card from your play area or from your hand, or take 1 damage.

name: Oppressive Mists
text: Revelation - Attach to your location. Limit 1 per location. Forced - After an investigator at this location draws 1 or more cards, that investigator must test [willpower] (3). If that investigator fails, he or she chooses and discards an equal number of cards. [action] [action]: Discard Oppressive Mists.

name: Violent Commands
text: Revelation - Put Violent Commands into play in your threat area. [action]: Deal 2 damage to an investigator at your location and discard Violent Commands. Forced - At the end of your turn: Test [willpower] (3). If you fail, take 1 horror.

name: Idle Hands
text: Revelation - Put Idle Hands into play in your threat area. [fast] If Idle Hands is in your threat area, during your turn, take 2 damage and discard Idle Hands: You may take an additional action this turn. Forced - At the end of your turn: Take 1 horror.

name: Need for Knowledge
text: Revelation - If you have no clues, Need for Knowledge gains surge. Otherwise, test [intellect] (X), where X equals the number of clues you have (to a maximum of 5). For each point you fail by, you must either take 1 horror or place 1 of your clues on your location.

name: Vassal of the Lurker
text: Hunter. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Each investigator at Vassal of the Lurker's location discards 1 card from the top of his or her deck.

name: Eldritch Accord
text: Peril. Revelation - You may draw 1 card. You may choose and discard up to 2 cards from your hand. Test [willpower] (X). If you fail, take 2 horror. X equals the number of cards in your hand when this test begins.

name: Bandolier
text: You have two additional hand slots, which can only be used to hold [[Weapon]] assets. While you have [[Weapon]] assets held in 2 or more hand slots, you get +1 [willpower].

name: Blackjack
text: [action]: Fight. You get +2 [combat] for this attack. If you perform this attack against an enemy engaged with another investigator, you deal +1 damage for this attack if you succeed, and no damage if you fail.

name: Preposterous Sketches
text: Play only if there is a clue on your location. Draw 3 cards.

name: Strange Solution
text: Researched. Uses (3 supplies). [action] Spend 1 supply and exhaust Strange Solution: Choose an investigator at your location to gain 2 resources and draw 1 card.

name: Contraband
text: Choose an asset controlled by an investigator at your location. Either (choose one): - Place 2 ammo or supply tokens on that asset and draw 1 card. - Double the number of ammo or supply tokens on that asset.

name: Think on Your Feet
text: Fast. Play when an enemy enters your location. Immediately move to a connecting location. (The enemy still enters your previous location.)

name: Rite of Seeking
text: Uses (3 charges). [action] Spend 1 charge: Investigate. Investigate using [willpower] instead of [intellect]. You get +2 [willpower] for this test. If you succeed you discover 1 additional clue at this location. If a [skull], [cultist], [tablet], [elder_thing], or [auto_fail] symbol is revealed during this test, after this test resolves lose all remaining actions and immediately end your turn.

name: Clarity of Mind
text: Uses (4 charges). [action] Spend 1 charge: Heal 2 horror from among investigators at your location.

name: Oops!
text: Fast. Play after you fail a skill test by 3 or less while attacking an enemy. Deal this attack's damage to any enemy at your location. If the attacked enemy is engaged with another investigator, this attack deals no damage to that investigator.

name: Rise to the Occasion
text: Commit only to a skill test you are performing, and only if the difficulty of that test is higher than your base skill value. Rise to the Occasion gets +X [wild] icons, where X is the difference between your base skill and this test's difficulty (to a maximum of +3 [wild] icons).

name: Through the Gates
text: Revelation - Draw the top card of your deck. If it is not a weakness, remove that card from the game. Then, search your deck, discard pile, hand, and all play areas for each other copy of that card you own, and remove them from the game, as well.

name: Return to Curtain Call
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Path to Carcosa Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Curtain Call shown here. - Construct the act deck using all six copies of Act 2a - "The Stranger." (Continued on reverse side.)

name: The Stranger
text: Objective - When The Man in the Pallid Mask would be discarded from play, advance.

name: The Stranger
text: Objective - When The Man in the Pallid Mask would be discarded from play, advance.

name: The Stranger
text: Objective - When The Man in the Pallid Mask would be discarded from play, advance.

name: Theatre Lounge
text: Forced - After you reveal the Theatre Lounge: Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck. Search the encounter deck for the 2 topmost hidden cards and secretly add both of those cards to your hand. Shuffle the encounter deck.

name: Prop Shop
text: While you are investigating Prop Shop, it gets -1 shroud for each horror on you. Forced - After you fail a skill test while investigating the Prop Shop: Take 1 horror.

name: La Comtesse
text: Spawn - Balcony. Hunter. Forced - After you defeat La Comtesse: Place her in your hand, as a hidden card. While La Comtesse is in your hand, she gains: "La Comtesse counts as 4 cards instead of 1 while checking your hand size. Forced - After you discard 1 or more cards from your hand during the upkeep phase: Take 1 horror."

name: Return to The Last King
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Path to Carcosa Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The Last King shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Sickening Reality
text: Each investigator at Dianne Devine's location takes 1 horror. Flip this card to its enemy side, and switch it with the asset version of Dianne Devine, removing that version from the game.

name: Dianne Devine
text: Prey - Most clues. Hunter. Dianne Devine gets +3 [per_investigator] health. Forced - After any investigator performs a parley action: Move Dianne Devine once toward that investigator.

name: Sickening Reality
text: If there are no Party Guests in play, place this card back underneath the scenario reference card and randomly choose a new Sickening Reality card to resolve. Otherwise, read the following: A chorus of laughter erupts nearby as one of the guests dances to the bizarre music. You head over to discover what is so funny, but what you find is no laughing matter at all. The guest’s dance partner lies sprawled on the floor, limp and bloodied, a gaping, gory hole in their chest. The guest turns to you with a disturbing look in their eye and a wicked grin spreading on their lips. Choose a Party Guest in play. Each investigator at that Party Guest's location takes 1 damage. Place 1 doom on the current agenda. Flip this card to its enemy side, and switch it with the chosen version of Party Guest, removing that version from the game.

name: Crazed Guest
text: Hunter. [action]: Parley. Test [intellect] (4). If you succeed, you manage to get through to them for just a moment; automatically evade Crazed Guest. If you fail, your words fall on deaf ears.

name: Dianne Devine
text: Each investigator at Dianne Devine's location cannot discover or take control of clues. Forced - At the beginning of the enemy phase: Find the [[Bystander]] asset with the fewest clues on it (among [[Bystander]] assets with at least one clue). Move Dianne Devine to that asset's location, if able.

name: Party Guest
text: Revelation - Find the [[Bystander]] asset with the most clues on it, and put Party Guest into play at that asset's location. Investigators cannot parley with [[Bystander]] assets at Party Guest's location. [action] Spend 2 resources: Parley. Move Party Guest to a connecting location.

name: Shocking Display
text: Revelation - Randomly resolve one of the Sickening Reality cards underneath the scenario reference card. Cannot be canceled.

name: Return to Echoes of the Past
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Path to Carcosa Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Echoes of the Past shown here. - Randomly choose one [[Basement]] Historical Society location and remove it from the game, without looking at its revealed side. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Quiet Halls
text: Entry Hall is connected to Quiet Halls. Quiet Halls is connected to each other [[Basement]] location. [action] If each location in play is revealed and there are no clues on locations in play: Place 1 [per_investigator] clues on Quiet Halls (from the token bank) .

name: Historical Society
text: While you have at least 1 hidden card in your hand, you cannot investigate this location.

name: Historical Society
text: Forced - When you investigate this location, if there is at least 1 [[Cultist]] enemy in play: This location gets -3 shroud for this investigation. If a [skull], [cultist], [tablet], [elder_thing], or [auto_fail] symbol is revealed during this test, move 1 clue from this location to the [[Cultist]] enemy farthest from you (before resolving the effects of the test) .

name: Historical Society
text: Forced - After you discover 1 or more clues at this location: One at a time, draw that many cards from the top of the encounter deck.

name: Keeper of the Oath
text: While the number of the current act is greater than the number of the current agenda, Keeper of the Oath gains hunter. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Find each investigator whose location shares a [[Trait]] with Keeper of the Oath's location. Move 1 clue from each of those investigators to Keeper of the Oath.

name: Return to The Unspeakable Oath
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Path to Carcosa Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to The Unspeakable Oath shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: "The Really Bad Ones" (v. III)
text: Ignore the text on the unrevealed side of [[Arkham Asylum]] locations. Objective - Find the Patient Confinement location with Daniel Chesterfield. (You will be instructed when to advance.)

name: "The Really Bad Ones" (v. IV)
text: Ignore the text on the unrevealed side of [[Arkham Asylum]] locations. Objective - Find the Patient Confinement location with Daniel Chesterfield. (You will be instructed when to advance.)

name: Host of Insanity
text: Massive. Hunter. Host of Insanity gets +2 [per_investigator] health. [action] If you control Clasp of Black Onyx: Parley. You reveal the clasp to the creature. It freezes in place, whispering a garbled prayer. Automatically evade Host of Insanity.

name: Radical Treatment
text: Forced - At the end of the round: Daniel Chesterfield's controller takes 2 horror. [action] If you are at Infirmary: Test [willpower] or [intellect] (6). If you succeed, detach Radical Treatment and add it to the victory display. If you control Clasp of Black Onyx, reduce the difficulty of this test by 3.

name: Clouded Memory
text: Peril. Revelation - You must either (choose one): - Forget something an investigator has "remembered" from an ability on a location. (It is no longer true.) - Take 1 horror and Clouded Memory gains surge.

name: Return to A Phantom of Truth
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Path to Carcosa Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to A Phantom of Truth shown here. - Randomly choose 1 of the 2 Montparnasse locations, 1 of the 2 Grand Guignol locations, 1 of the 2 Père Lachaise Cemetery locations... (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Montparnasse
text: Forced - After you discover any number of clues from Montparnasse: Discard cards from your hand with that many [willpower] icons among them. If you cannot, take 1 horror.

name: Grand Guignol
text: [action] Spend 5 resources: Either place 1 doom on the current agenda, or remove 1 doom from the current agenda. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Père Lachaise Cemetery
text: Forced - After an enemy moves into Père Lachaise Cemetery: It gets +1 horror value for the remainder of the round.

name: Canal Saint-Martin
text: While you are at Canal Saint-Martin, you must use your [combat] in place of your [agility], and vice versa.

name: Notre-Dame
text: Each enemy at Notre-Dame gets +1 fight and -1 evade. [reaction] After you successfully evade an enemy at Notre-Dame by 3 or more during your turn: You may take an additional action this turn. (Group limit once per round.)

name: Gardens of Luxembourg
text: Forced - After you enter Gardens of Luxembourg, if there is at least 1 [[Byakhee]] enemy in play: Reveal a random token from the chaos bag. If you reveal a [skull], [cultist], [tablet], [elder_thing], or [auto_fail] symbol, each [[Byakhee]] enemy in play readies and moves to Gardens of Luxembourg.

name: Figure in the Shadows
text: Revelation - Check Campaign Log. - If you have more (or equal) Doubt than Conviction , move The Organist 1 location away from the nearest investigator (to a location with no investigators, if possible). - If you have more Conviction than Doubt , resolve The Organist's hunter keyword.

name: Return to The Pallid Mask
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Path to Carcosa Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter set for Return to The Pallid Mask shown here. - When building the Catacombs deck, first set aside the Tomb of Shadows and the Blocked Passage, as normal. - Next, add the 4 new Catacombs locations included in this encounter set with the remaining Catacombs locations, and determine your starting location, as normal. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Secret Passage
text: [action] If you control Clasp of Black Onyx: You place the clasp within the skull's eye socket, and the passage opens. Reveal the Catacombs location to the right of Secret Passage. Forced - When Secret Passage is revealed: Put the topmost Catacombs in the Catacombs deck into play to the right of Secret Passage.

name: Mound of Bones
text: Forced - When Mound of Bones is revealed: Put the top 4 Catacombs in the Catacombs deck into play above, below, to the left, and to the right of Mound of Bones. At the end of the current round, search the encounter deck and discard pile for a Malformed Skeleton and spawn it at Mound of Bones.

name: Research Site
text: [reaction] After you successfully investigate Research Site: Reveal any Catacombs location. (Group limit once per game.) Forced - When Research Site is revealed: Put the topmost Catacombs in the Catacombs deck into play above, below, or to the right of any other location.

name: Sea of Skulls
text: Forced - After you end your turn at Sea of Skulls: You must either take 1 direct horror or choose and discard 3 cards from your hand. Forced - When Sea of Skulls is revealed: Put the topmost Catacombs in the Catacombs deck into play above, below, or to the right of the location farthest from Sea of Skulls and mark it with a horror token. For the remainder of the game, Sea of Skulls is connected to the marked location.

name: Malformed Skeleton
text: Hunter. Forced - When Malformed Skeleton would resolve its hunter keyword: If there are no investigators within 2 locations of Malformed Skeleton, instead of resolving its hunter keyword, move it to the Catacombs location nearest to an investigator. Forced - When Malformed Skeleton attacks you: It deals either its damage or its horror, instead of both.

name: Return to Black Stars Rise
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Path to Carcosa Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Black Stars Rise shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Cloister
text: [action] If there are no clues on Cloister: Parley. Test [willpower] or [intellect] (4). If you succeed, remember that you have "found the tower key."

name: Knight's Hall
text: [action] If there are no clues on Knight's Hall: Test [combat] or [agility] (3). If you succeed, remember that you have "found a guide."

name: Hastur's Gaze
text: Peril. Hidden. Revelation - Secretly add Hastur's Gaze to your hand. Forced - After 1 or more doom is placed on the "c" agenda: Take 2 horror and discard Hastur's Gaze from your hand.

name: Hastur's Grasp
text: Peril. Hidden. Revelation - Secretly add Hastur's Grasp to your hand. Forced - After 1 or more doom is placed on the "a" agenda: Take 2 damage and discard Hastur's Grasp from your hand.

name: Return to Dim Carcosa
text: Perform the setup as indicated in The Path to Carcosa Campaign Guide, with the following exceptions: - When gathering encounter sets, also gather the new encounter sets for Return to Dim Carcosa shown here. (Continued on reverse side.)

name: Palace of the King
text: Palace of the King cannot be flipped over while Hastur has more than 2 [per_investigator] remaining health.

name: Hastur's Last Stand
